Chromium Daily Summaries

Latest Chromium commits summarized daily

📅 View Weekly Summaries →

2026-03-07

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-03-07

Overview

This digest covers 100 commits from the Chromium repository, from commit (01b8595) to (85b4f29). Key changes include a significant behavioral update to fullscreen mode for iframes, the introduction of the SurfaceEmbedConnector API for advanced WebUI embedding, and the removal of several feature flags as features become stable. This summary focuses on changes impacting browser behavior, APIs, extensions, and security, filtering out platform-specific (Android, iOS, Linux, ChromeOS) and web-platform-internal (Blink, V8) modifications.

Browser Behavior

  • ⚠️ BREAKING Fullscreen mode will now be exited when a subframe that requested fullscreen (or contains the fullscreen element) performs a cross-document navigation. Previously, only main frame navigations would exit fullscreen. This change in WebContents behavior may impact applications that rely on iframes navigating without losing the browser's fullscreen state. (9f026d2)
  • A crash in browser_navigator.cc has been fixed by changing how a new tab's opener is determined, now relying exclusively on source_contents to avoid issues when the opener is not a tab. (5f43eeb)
  • The feature flag for PWA navigation capturing with scope extensions (kPwaNavigationCapturingWithScopeExtensions) has been removed, solidifying this as stable behavior. (a3f9929)

New Features & APIs

  • Introduces the new public API SurfaceEmbedConnector in content/public/browser. This API enables embedding a child WebContents within a parent without a traditional frame hierarchy, designed for advanced embedding scenarios like the WebUI Browser Surface project. (a01932c) (70e95fb)
  • The WebNN API Origin Trial has been re-enabled for M-147 after being disabled due to security issues. (bd34687)
  • Internal AI APIs have been refactored to improve language support handling, and a new flag has been added for a Summarizer API powered by Gemini Nano. (5f7603d)

Security & Networking

  • The feature flag for multicast in the Direct Sockets API (kMulticastInDirectSockets) has been removed. The feature has been stable and enabled by default since M144. (85b4f29)
  • A bug in Device Bound Session Cookies (DBSC) garbage collection has been fixed, preventing the premature deletion of valid keys not yet loaded into memory. (bc1b8c9)
  • The feature flag kUsePostBodyForUrlProvisionFetcher has been removed, making POST requests the permanent and only method for media provisioning fetches. (dd130db)

Extensions

  • GuestViewBase and its subclasses (MimeHandlerViewGuest, WebViewGuest) have been refactored to simplify how pinch-to-zoom and double-tap-to-zoom gestures are disabled, now using SetIgnoreZoomGestures instead of intercepting events. (bc8afa5)
  • An internal, unused enum value ServiceWorkerState::BrowserState::kReady has been removed from the service worker state machine within the extensions browser code. (2dacc81)

Infrastructure & Internals

  • The //chrome/browser/favicon component has been split into public (:favicon) and implementation (:impl) targets to improve dependency management and clarify its public API surface. (cad118d)
  • A use-after-free bug in the Prompt API has been fixed by ensuring a callback that could delete its owner is posted to the message queue instead of being run synchronously. (f2f5a92)
  • Code for the experimental and never-shipped ElementInternals.type feature has been completely removed. (3ba8c47)
  • The logic for beforeunload handling in RenderFrameHostImpl was refactored to use GlobalRenderFrameHostId instead of raw pointers, preventing potential use-after-free bugs during complex navigation scenarios. (da47bac)

2026-03-06

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-03-06

Overview

This summary covers 565 relevant commits out of 741 total commits on the main branch for March 6th, 2026. The day saw a large number of breaking changes across core APIs, UI components, and testing infrastructure, requiring significant updates for embedders. Notable improvements include the ability for WebSockets to use HTTP/3 connections and the introduction of a Rust-based security policy component. The first relevant commit was (2b3bc52) and the last was (485e8be).

Core APIs & Libraries

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The raw pointer overloads for base::File::Read and base::File::Write have been made private. Code must be updated to use the base::span overloads for file I/O operations. (2f6529b)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The callback for the public FedCM API content::webid::FederatedEmbedderLoginRequest::Set has been changed from a base::RepeatingCallback to a base::OnceCallback. Callers must be updated as the continuation state is now considered terminal. (2d27c87)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The deprecated kPepper value has been removed from the public media::MediaContentType enum. Any remaining code referencing this value will fail to compile. (57f8280)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The kEnableWebGLImageChromium command-line switch has been moved from content/public to third_party/blink/public, and the kDisableWebGLImageChromium switch has been removed entirely. Embedders referencing these switches will need to update their paths or remove usage. (e327feb) (8854092)
  • A new Rust implementation of ChildProcessSecurityPolicy has been introduced behind a feature flag. This is a major step in migrating core security components to Rust for improved memory safety. (5440cf2)

Browser & UI

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public TabRendererData class has been completely removed. Custom UI components and embedder code must be updated to use the TabData struct instead. (bf0fd12) (af1897a)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The --launch-isolated command-line switch for Windows browser isolation has been removed. This feature is now configured via the Windows Registry, and a new C++ API chrome::SetIsolationState has been introduced for programmatic control. (03a70ad)
  • The "Auto Tab Groups" feature has been removed from the Tab Search WebUI. (549edd6)
  • A new command-line flag, --translate-target-language, has been added to automatically translate pages to a specified language upon loading. (d23ab77)
  • The bookmarks overflow menu will now remain open after deleting an item, improving user workflow. (a324040)
  • On macOS, a stability issue with re-entrant calls to TextInputClientMac has been fixed by returning a dummy value instead of crashing. (7ab3e5c)

Security, Sync & Identity

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The signature of the virtual method syncer::DataTypeController::GetPreconditionState() has been changed. Embedders with custom DataTypeController implementations must update their method signature to GetPreconditionState(const PreconditionContext& context). (37a2d3f)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public DiceResponseParams::SigninInfo structure has been modified to support multiple accounts. Embedders who parse DICE sign-in responses or interact directly with this data structure will need to update their code. (740a888)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The signature of the public Safe Browsing method ClientSideDetectionHost::ReportUnsafeSite has changed. It no longer accepts raw screenshot data and now requires a single SkBitmap object. (a0748d0)
  • Data Controls policies have been extended to cover custom clipboard formats, allowing enterprise policies to block or modify data during paste operations. (1a34b1d)
  • A potential Out-Of-Memory (OOM) crash in the History Embeddings feature was fixed by adding a size check before decompressing gzip data, preventing issues with corrupted files. (3dcee0a)
  • On macOS, a bug causing Device Bound Session Cookies (DBSC) to hit quota limits after system sleep was fixed by migrating quota tracking from base::TimeTicks to base::Time. (25b61d1)

Extensions

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The signature for ExtensionTabUtil::IsTabStripEditable() has been changed to IsTabStripEditable(Profile& profile). All callers must be updated to pass the relevant profile. (8bf2b94)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public static method ExtensionTabUtil::OpenOptionsPageFromAPI() has been removed. Its logic has been moved into the chrome.runtime API implementation, breaking any external code that called this utility function directly. (1567ebe)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The structure of the RequestFileSourceResponse dictionary in the chrome.developerPrivate extension API has changed. Source code details are now nested within a source object, which will break tools that parse the previous flat structure. (5dd40e6)
  • A heap-use-after-free crash in the declarativeContent.removeRules API has been fixed. The crash occurred when duplicate rule identifiers were provided. (98a65b0)

Networking & Developer Tools

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The Audits.checkContrast command has been removed from the DevTools protocol. Developer tools or automation scripts that use this command for accessibility checks will no longer function. (f0f700c)
  • A new WS_OVER_H3 job type was added to HttpStreamFactory, allowing WebSockets to reuse existing QUIC (HTTP/3) connections. This can significantly improve connection performance and reduce latency. (9c623b4)
  • A memory regression in the Network Service has been fixed by correcting the memory accounting logic for Durable Messages in DevTools to use actual decompressed byte counts. (ff728ac)
  • A new "Export to JSON" button has been added to the chrome://safe-browsing page for easier debugging and data analysis. (5ed885e)

Remoting

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The remoting signaling API has been refactored. FTL (Federated Transfer Layer) specific methods have been moved from the generic SignalStrategy public interface to a new, dedicated FtlSignalStrategy class. (f154eb3)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: A method in the public remoting::SignalStrategy::Listener interface has been renamed from OnSignalStrategyStateChange to OnSignalingStateChanged. All implementations must be updated. (88d4a31)

Testing & Infrastructure

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The BrowserActivator test utility class has been removed. Tests that relied on this class for managing browser window activation must be updated to use views::test::MockWidgetActivator. (b6ec3b4)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The ui/base/interaction API has been refactored to use a strongly-typed UniqueIdentifier class instead of the ElementIdentifier alias. This changes multiple public function signatures in ElementTracker and related classes, breaking embedders who use this UI testing framework. (3ac974d) (fe6930d)

2026-03-05

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-03-05

Overview

This summary covers 724 commits on the main branch for March 5th, 2026, with 544 identified as relevant to developers building on Chromium. The changes span from commit (9fa596c) to (605a489). This digest highlights numerous breaking API changes in core libraries, extensions, networking, and autofill, requiring immediate attention from embedders.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The core content::ContentBrowserClient interface has been modified. The method GetPermissionsPolicyForIsolatedWebApp was removed and replaced by SupportsBaselinePermissionsPolicyForIsolatedApp and GetBaselinePermissionsPolicyForIsolatedApp. Embedders must update their ContentBrowserClient implementation. (fc322bb)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING A new pure virtual function, ShowAutofillPopupWithBnpl, has been added to the autofill::AutofillClient interface. Embedders with custom AutofillClient implementations must add this new method to support "Buy Now, Pay Later" UI flows. (cc231b0)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The stable chrome.tabs.update extension API will now throw an error when an extension attempts to un-highlight all tabs in a window. Previously, this was silently ignored, and extensions relying on the old behavior may break. (ebae198)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING In the //base library, the base::File::ReadAtCurrentPos(char* data, int size) method has been made private. Callers must migrate to the public overload that accepts a base::span. (3b61a3b)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The memory monitoring utility base::AvailablePhysicalMemoryMonitor has been renamed to base::AvailableMemoryMonitor. Embedders using this class must update class names, includes, and access to the renamed fields within its MemorySample struct. (0ed195d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The has_sha1 field has been removed from the public net::CertVerifyResult structure as part of SHA-1 deprecation efforts. Code directly inspecting this field for certificate verification will fail to compile. (70a730f)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public class ExtensionWebUI, used for managing extension URL overrides, has been renamed to ExtensionUrlOverrides. Embedders must update includes from extension_web_ui.h to extension_url_overrides.h and use the new class name. (10f8396)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The remoting::signaling::SignalStrategy::Listener interface was modified. The OnIncomingMessage method was removed and replaced with OnIncomingStanza, requiring client updates. (bbbcf8a)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public static method ExtensionTabUtil::CreateTabList() has been removed. External projects using this utility function will experience a build failure. (98f3bdd)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The virtual method ContentRendererClient::WaitForProcessReady() has been removed from the public API. Embedders implementing this method will have a build failure. (74b27cf)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The require_metadata_verdict field was removed from the public ContentAnalysisRequest protobuf message for enterprise connectors. Embedders using this field will need to update their code. (0cb8117)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The destination_address parameter has been removed from the public remoting::SignalStrategy::SendMessage method. Callers must now include this information within the SignalingMessage object. (21be984)

Extensions

  • A use-after-free bug in global shortcut handling was fixed by using a WeakPtr-bound callback, preventing crashes when an extension with global shortcuts is unloaded. (fd29b2e)
  • New UMA metrics have been introduced to monitor the health of the chrome.storage.local LevelDB backend, tracking operation status and repair success rates. (63aef2c)
  • A security vulnerability causing a browser crash on extraction of malformed .tar or .tar.xz archives has been fixed with stricter validation. (2f05465)
  • Fixed a crash that could occur when retrieving an extension action's tooltip on a native page like the New Tab Page by adding a null check for the WebContents. (51b479c)

Networking & Security

  • The chrome-search:// scheme has been hardened by registering it as a 'web-safe isolated scheme', preventing compromised non-instant renderers from navigating to it. (74b27cf)
  • A new method, NetworkDelegate::ShouldForceIgnoreSiteForCookies(), has been introduced to allow Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) refresh requests from extensions to bypass SameSite cookie restrictions. (9e66171)
  • A new feature, kEnableChromeSchemeURLBlocklistException, automatically creates an exception for the chrome:// scheme when a wildcard (*) is used in the URLBlocklist policy, preventing accidental blocking of critical internal pages. (b7792df)
  • The network service on Windows is now more resilient to crashes. It keeps the process handle of a crashed service alive briefly to prevent socket assignment errors if the OS reuses the process ID quickly. (857bbbf)

Core & Base Library

  • A new utility, base::reinterpret_span<T>(), has been added to safely reinterpret a span of bytes as a span of a specific type T, providing a safer alternative to reinterpret_cast. (9a040b8)
  • A heap-use-after-free vulnerability in base::OnceCallbackList has been fixed by deferring list mutations during re-entrant Notify() calls until the outermost iteration completes. (36acd49)
  • A use-after-move bug in ThreadGroupImpl::WorkerDelegate::SwapProcessedTask was fixed, improving the reliability of the task scheduler's idle notifications. (021b0f3)

Browser UI & Features

  • To prevent acting on stale data, a sequence_id has been added to AutocompleteResult and is now passed to the Omnibox OpenPopupSelection mojom call for verification. (64d69ae)
  • The legacy discounts page action icon and its controlling feature flag, kEnableDiscountsInPageAction, have been removed to standardize the discounts UI. (bf4e3c1)
  • Enterprise badging on the New Tab Page footer is now standard behavior, as the kEnterpriseBadgingForNtpFooter feature flag has been removed. (5192a39)
  • The "Close" button in the Reading Mode side panel has been relabeled to "Close reading mode" for improved accessibility and clarity. (cb7d075)

Developer Tools & Testing

  • A new convenience function, content::SimulateCharTyped(), has been added to content/public/test/browser_test_utils.h to simplify simulating character input in browser tests. (ec38710)
  • DevTools AI assistance features are being migrated from the AIDA API to the new Gemini Code Assist (GCA) API, controlled by the kDevToolsUseGcaApi feature flag. (057018c)
  • Support for the Emulation.updateScreen() DevTools protocol method has been added to Headless mode on macOS. (ab05c35)

2026-03-04

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-03-04

Overview

This summary covers 527 relevant commits out of 712 total commits to the Chromium main branch on March 4, 2026. The changes span from the first commit (5ce3994) to the last commit (91ea801).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The core clipboard API ui::Clipboard::GetStandardFormats in ui/base/clipboard/clipboard.h has been converted from a synchronous function to an asynchronous one that requires a callback. This is a major signature change that will break any code directly calling this method. (14c828a)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public virtual method CreateURLLoaderHandlerForServiceWorkerNavigationPreload in content/public/browser/content_browser_client.h has been renamed to CreateURLLoaderHandlerForServiceWorkerInitiatedNavigationRequest. Embedders overriding this method must update to the new name. (66509a9)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The default behavior of the public API WebContents::IgnoreInputEvents has changed. The single-argument version now blocks accessibility input events by default, which may break embedders relying on the previous behavior. (c208fa7)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The deprecated, non-spanified Pickle::WriteBytes(const void* data, size_t length) overload has been removed from the public API in base/pickle.h. Code must be updated to use the span-based overload. (18a2af4) (4fda9b2)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Public virtual methods in ContentRendererClient and the ServiceWorkerContext::StartWorkerCallback signature have changed to include a blink::ServiceWorkerToken. Embedders implementing ContentRendererClient or using StartWorkerForScope must update their method signatures. (39a3c85)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public API for side panel content creation in chrome/browser/ui/side_panel/side_panel_entry.h has been changed. GetContent() and CacheView() now use a SidePanelNativeView type alias instead of std::unique_ptr<views::View>, requiring updates for consumers. (e769dc6)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public method DataProtectionUrlLookupService::DoLookup now requires a SessionID and no longer accepts a content::WebContents*. Consumers of this enterprise security service must update their calls. (9736f5a)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING On Windows, the chrome::IsolatedBrowser class has been removed from the public API and replaced with a free function chrome::LaunchIsolatedBrowser, which has a different signature and return type. (18f98f7)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The FedCM API has been refactored, moving FederatedActorLoginRequest to content/public/browser and renaming it. A new method was also added to the public content::webid::IdentityCredentialSource interface, breaking existing implementations. (5c44bb8)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public method RemovePartialSplits in chrome/browser/ui/tabs/tab_strip_model.h has been renamed to MaybeRemoveSplitsForUpdate. Projects interacting with the tab strip model for split tabs must adopt the new name. (efeb792)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The Tab Declutter feature has been removed, including its associated public command IDs (IDC_DECLUTTER_TAB, IDC_DECLUTTER_MENU) from chrome/app/chrome_command_ids.h. This is a public API removal. (5bbd8d1)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The kOpenSplitView command has been removed from the public mojom interface ui/webui/resources/js/browser_command/browser_command.mojom. Any WebUI or feature relying on this command will break. (98d1b49)

APIs and Core Infrastructure

  • A new TabDataManager class has been introduced to centralize observation of tab data changes, simplifying how UI clients can listen for and react to updates. (56faad1)
  • In Autofill, a new method Autofill[Driver|Agent]::ScrollFieldIntoView() was added to programmatically bring a form field into view. (0176466)
  • On macOS, openURL has been made asynchronous to prevent blocking the main thread when launching external applications, improving browser responsiveness. (3588647)
  • Third-party dependencies were updated, including zlib to version 1.3.2, abseil-cpp, and QUICHE. (2b1b62f) (2383140) (c849809)
  • On Windows, a new feature flag has been added to retry CreateFileMapping upon ERROR_COMMITMENT_LIMIT failures, potentially improving stability under memory pressure. (9c86acc)

Browser UI and Features

  • Vertical tabs received several improvements, including better contrast in dark mode, drop target link arrows for drag-and-drop, and animations for adding/removing tabs. (bc90d82) (45ea30f) (fc40d95)
  • A use-after-free crash on macOS related to rendering tab group icons in the system menu has been fixed by removing a cached pointer to the ColorProvider. (f6620ae)
  • The profile creation flow has been updated with a visually refreshed customization bubble and a new sign-in promo for the avatar button in the toolbar. (fb5b7ad) (5cd1c18) (1802dbe)
  • A bug in 'Send Tab To Self' that caused devices with generic names (e.g., "iPhone") to be improperly deduplicated has been fixed, ensuring all target devices are displayed. (be489af)
  • The old 'Delete Browsing Data' settings page has been completely removed from the codebase, finalizing the transition to the newer UI. (ea17fef)

Extensions

  • Extensions can now set the Origin header in fetch requests. This behavior is controlled by the kBypassRequestForbiddenHeadersCheck feature flag and is not enabled by default. (0970f34)
  • A new enterprise policy, ExtensionDOMActivityLoggingEnabled, has been defined to control telemetry for extension DOM activity and script injection. (20089d3) (06c68e0)

Networking and Security

  • A new feature flag, kDohFallbackAllowedWithLocalNameservers, has been added to allow DNS-over-HTTPS to fall back to insecure DNS even when local nameservers are configured, intended for testing purposes. (1e172fc)
  • Safe Browsing's Client-Side Detection (CSD) logic was updated to bypass allowlists and report limits for user-initiated reports, ensuring users can always report a suspicious page. (82a0029)
  • A response header monitor has been implemented for prerendered pages, introducing new methods to PrerenderHandle to track prewarm progress. (86eb608)

Testing

  • The HistogramTester utility has a new helper method, GetTotalCountForPrefix, for easier testing of related histograms. (f4020c3)
  • A new method, SetWaitForNextFrame, was added to RenderFrameSubmissionObserver to provide a more reliable way to wait for the next frame in browser tests. (eb37403)
  • The test-only MockActivationController was relanded to better emulate widget activation in tests on Windows and macOS, improving test reliability. (8d74846)

2026-03-03

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-03-03

Overview

This summary covers 339 commits on the main branch for March 3rd, 2026, with 247 commits filtered as relevant to developers working on Chromium-based projects. The changes span from the first commit (7d231e6)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((7d231e6)) to the last (d17b81a)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((d17b81a)) and include several breaking changes to C++ and Mojo APIs in the networking, extensions, and media stacks.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The chrome.browser API for Chrome Platform Apps has been removed from non-ChromeOS builds (Windows, Mac, Linux). Any remaining apps using this API on these platforms will break. (d1392f5)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The base class for extensions::IconImage::Observer and extensions::ExtensionPrefsObserver has been changed to base::CheckedObserver. Downstream C++ code must update observer implementations to inherit from the new base class and override the virtual destructor. (95f9642)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The return type of the public virtual method net::TransportConnectJob::Factory::Create was changed from std::unique_ptr<TransportConnectJob> to std::unique_ptr<ConnectJob>. Embedders with custom factory implementations must update their method signatures. (da27f3d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Support for enabling SHA-1 signed certificates for local trust anchors has been removed. The VERIFY_ENABLE_SHA1_LOCAL_ANCHORS flag was removed from net::CertVerifyProc::VerifyFlags and the enable_sha1_local_anchors field was removed from net::CertVerifier::Config and its Mojo equivalent. (0533a59) (5b62b90)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The strides field in the public Mojo struct media::mojom::SharedMemoryVideoFrameData was changed from array<int32> to array<uint32>. Code serializing or deserializing this structure must be updated. (d0a80fe)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The signature of the public method media::VideoEncoderInfo::DoesSupportGpuSharedImages was changed to require a gpu::SharedImageUsageSet parameter, adding a new requirement for checking encoder capabilities. (3e781fb)

Extensions

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The chrome.browser API for Chrome Platform Apps has been removed from Windows, Mac, and Linux builds as part of the ongoing app deprecation. It is now exclusive to ChromeOS. (d1392f5)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The C++ observer classes extensions::IconImage::Observer and extensions::ExtensionPrefsObserver now inherit from base::CheckedObserver to improve memory safety. Downstream code must update observer implementations to match the new base class. (95f9642) (2388bb8)
  • Initial data structures and proto definitions were added to support entitlement enforcement for Isolated Web Apps (IWAs), laying the groundwork for future feature allowlisting. (1929c2e)

Security

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: Support for SHA-1 signed certificates for locally trusted anchors has been removed entirely. The VERIFY_ENABLE_SHA1_LOCAL_ANCHORS flag and enable_sha1_local_anchors config option have been deleted from the net library and its Mojo interfaces. (0533a59) (5b62b90)
  • A Time-of-check to time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerability allowing UI spoofing via popups was fixed. A visibility check is now performed immediately before showing a popup, preventing compromised renderers in background tabs from creating them. (948f7d1)
  • To prevent potential heap information disclosure, memory allocated for software video decoders (libvpx, dav1d) is now zero-initialized. (c0244ca)
  • Navigation interception for FedCM is now correctly disabled when opening a new tab with target="_blank" and rel="noopener", fixing an issue on certain websites. (fd5261f)

Networking

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The net::TransportConnectJob::Factory::Create method signature has changed. It now returns a std::unique_ptr<ConnectJob> to support different job types for features like Happy Eyeballs V2. Embedders implementing this factory must update their code. (da27f3d)
  • The SQLite-based persistent disk cache was optimized to use sqlite3_bind_blob instead of incremental I/O, improving performance for insert operations. (abd3492)
  • A crash in the SQL disk cache caused by double-counting an entry during eviction has been fixed. (c463655)
  • An optimization was added to bypass the ServiceWorker synthetic response path when a navigation is intercepted by an embedder-level URLLoaderFactory, such as for Search Prefetch. (3c79032)

Media

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: In the media Mojo API, the strides field within media::mojom::SharedMemoryVideoFrameData was changed from a signed 32-bit integer array to an unsigned one. Consumers of this interface must update their data types. (d0a80fe)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The C++ method media::VideoEncoderInfo::DoesSupportGpuSharedImages now requires a gpu::SharedImageUsageSet parameter to perform stricter checks on encoder capabilities. (3e781fb)

Browser UI & Internals

  • On Windows, a bug in text fields where Ctrl+Right Arrow word navigation would incorrectly skip over punctuation before a newline has been fixed. (b4df2e6)
  • The kUseDisplaySDRMaxLuminanceNits feature, related to HDR video playback, is now enabled by default only on Windows to prevent undesirable behavior on other platforms. (232238f)
  • A new context menu item has been added to editable fields (like the omnibox) to manually trigger the @memory search popup. (ee6b371)
  • The PrefStore::Observer::OnPrefValueChanged method was explicitly marked to allow re-entrancy, clarifying its contract for observers. (8bec30a)
  • Code health improvement: FileUtilitiesHostImpl was updated to use the strongly-typed content::ChildProcessId instead of a raw integer, improving type safety. (42c876b)

Build & Testing

  • A public abseil crc target has been added to the Abseil build configuration, allowing third-party code within a Chromium build to depend on it directly. (8ebb498)
  • Microsoft has been added as a recognized GPU vendor in the GPU test framework, enabling the creation of vendor-specific test expectations. (8b74400)

2026-03-02

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-03-02

Overview

This digest covers 607 commits from the main branch on March 2nd, 2026, with 457 identified as relevant to developers working on Chromium-based projects for Windows and macOS. The day was marked by a significant number of breaking changes, particularly the move to a fully asynchronous Clipboard API and the removal of several public classes and methods. The first and last commits in this range are (b8d1bad) and (c3cd25d) respectively.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The ui::Clipboard API is now fully asynchronous. The synchronous methods ReadAvailableTypes, ReadText, ReadAsciiText, ReadBookmark, and ReadData have been removed from the public interface. All consumers must migrate to the asynchronous, callback-based versions. (1d23bcd) (34bc314) (c6bd1aa) (a1527fc) (8a2045e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public helper class BrowserListEnumerator, used for iterating through the BrowserList, has been removed. Alternative iteration patterns must be used. (eaf9b1a)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The base::MemoryPressureMonitor class and its static Get() accessor have been removed. Embedders must now use the MemoryPressureListener API. Additionally, the deprecated MemoryPressureListenerTags enum has been deleted. (1acb6aa) (dad691e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The virtual method GetAnchorView() was removed from the public ToolbarButtonProvider interface. Implementations must be updated to override GetBubbleAnchor(), which returns a views::BubbleAnchor instead of a views::View*. (e8f13b0)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The signature of net::FileStream::Seek has changed. Its callback now uses base::expected<int64_t, net::Error> instead of Int64CompletionOnceCallback, requiring updates to all callers. (e6d8aba)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The AppBannerManager class hierarchy was refactored. AppBannerManagerDesktop no longer inherits from AppBannerManager, which will break code relying on this inheritance for casting or polymorphism. (f3143ea)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The ExtensionsMenuViewModel::Observer::OnActionIconUpdated method signature was changed to include the action's index, breaking existing observer implementations. (2411cda)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public API of SadTabView has been altered, and its friend class declaration has been removed from the core ui/views/view.h header. (fc0af00)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Several GPU and graphics APIs were changed: accelerated image decoding interfaces were removed from gpu_info.mojom, an overload of SharedImageFactory::UpdateSharedImage was removed, gfx::BufferPlane was deleted, and the signature of GLOzone::ImportNativePixmap was changed. (54c67d2) (0a6652b) (2f15cde)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The entire chrome/browser/follow feature and the WebAuthn CaBLE v1 protocol implementation have been removed from the codebase. (df23f4f) (02514ec)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The extensions::BookmarksFunction::RunOnReady() method signature was changed to return ResponseAction instead of ResponseValue to support asynchronous clipboard operations. (a1527fc)

API & Core Interfaces

  • As a consequence of the asynchronous clipboard migration, the RenderViewContextMenu constructor signature was changed to accept an is_paste_enabled boolean. (1d23bcd)
  • The DomStorageDatabaseFactory was refactored, renaming its Open() method to Create() and changing its signature to return a base::SequenceBound object directly instead of using a callback. (3000068)
  • The reentrancy policy for several core UI observers, including CompositorObserver and NativeThemeObserver, was explicitly changed to allow reentrancy, which could alter observer behavior in complex UI scenarios. (dd1dfbf)
  • The Direct Sockets implementation was modularized from //chrome/browser into its own component at //chrome/browser/direct_sockets. (f9c102e)

Browser & UI

  • The Send Tab To Self feature was enhanced to propagate the user's scroll position to the receiving device. This is controlled by the kSendTabToSelfPropagateScrollPosition feature flag. (54ee74b)
  • The @memory Autofill feature was improved to correctly position the cursor and replace the specific @@ trigger that invoked the popup. (46944d2)
  • A new RestartabilityMonitor was introduced to gather metrics on what prevents Chrome from automatically restarting for pending updates. (a1db522)
  • The layout of the chrome://browser-switch/internals page was redesigned for better scaling. (4540a72)

Networking & Security

  • The DNS-over-HTTPS (DoH) logic was updated to require a canary domain check to pass before falling back to a secure provider, in addition to a DoH server being available. (ab614c3)
  • Support for Source-Specific Multicast (SSM) was added to UDPSocket via new JoinSourceGroup() and LeaveSourceGroup() methods. (c1ccae1)
  • Tracked preferences will now treat weakly encrypted hashes as invalid if a stronger key is available, enhancing preference security. (92f4fbc)
  • The TcpConnectJob was updated to more accurately report DNS and connection timing metrics, improving performance diagnostics. (16789da)

Performance & Memory

  • The ReportUkm and SynchronizedScrolling features have been disabled by default. (d6a9743) (4ac3496)
  • On Windows, new metrics were added to track how often system memory lists are low, providing better insight into memory pressure. (e724ff2)
  • New metrics were introduced to measure the duration of "zero-disruption" opportunities (e.g., no windows open, screen locked) where Chrome could restart for an update without interrupting the user. (4298304)

2026-03-01

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-03-01

Overview

This digest covers 3 selected commits out of an initial 10, focusing on changes relevant to browser UI, features, and core components, while excluding updates to the Blink engine, testing, and localization. No major breaking changes to stable public APIs were identified. Highlights include a UI bug fix for the Composebox component, a modification to a feature behind a flag, and a stability improvement in the preference management system.

Browser UI & Features

  • Fixed autocomplete query logic within the composebox UI. This change ensures autocomplete suggestions are correctly managed when adding or removing 'autotab' chips, preventing incorrect suggestion popups and improving layout spacing. (002ced6)
  • The same-site restriction for Asynchronous Page Classification (APC) has been removed to address a bug. This change is gated by a feature flag and does not alter the default stable behavior. (b04c306)

Core Components

  • Observer notifications in JsonPrefStore and TestingPrefStore were updated to explicitly allow re-entrancy for PrefStore::Observer::OnPrefValueChanged. This change formalizes an existing, albeit untracked, behavior to improve stability and prevent potential re-entrancy-related issues for embedders observing preference changes. (98cab97)

2026-02-28

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-02-28

Overview

This digest covers 66 commits from (da64ff6)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((da64ff6)) to (c1640e1)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((c1640e1)). After filtering for changes relevant to Windows/macOS embedders and ignoring experimental features, internal refactors, and platform-specific code, this summary highlights 11 key commits across Security, UI, Testing, Infrastructure, and Extensions. No major breaking changes to stable public APIs were identified.

Security & Networking

  • Browser-side validation has been added to enforce the allow-popups sandbox attribute. Previously checked only in the renderer, this change adds a check in RenderFrameHostImpl::CreateNewWindow to prevent sandboxed frames without this attribute from creating popups, hardening security. (a12e651)
  • For the experimental Connection-Allowlist feature, server-side redirects are now blocked for navigations. This change in NavigationRequest prevents navigations allowed via the allowlist from being redirected, with future work planned to add a redirection-allowed attribute. (da95243)

Browser UI & UX

  • Several UI bug fixes and code health improvements have been landed. A null pointer dereference was fixed in the accessibility utility AnnounceInActiveBrowser (48d70142...). A bug preventing collapsed tab groups from expanding during a drag operation was resolved in TabStrip (533d98e4...). On Windows, ui/views/win code was updated to prefix Win32 API calls with :: for better code health (afb46199...). (48d7014) (533d98e) (afb4619)
  • The Mojo API for the experimental WebUI Toolbar has been refactored for clarity. UI-specific methods like showContextMenu have been moved from components/browser_apis/browser_controls/browser_controls_api.mojom to a new, dedicated components/browser_apis/ui_controllers/toolbar/toolbar_ui_api.mojom interface. This affects the implementation of the feature but is not a breaking change to a stable API as the WebUI Toolbar is behind a feature flag. (06faf85)

Testing

  • The sandbox test runner API for Windows has been updated to be more ergonomic. A new RunTest method allows passing arguments directly instead of building a command-line string. The old API remains available for backward compatibility while existing tests are migrated. (f6e6490) (7b688cd)

Infrastructure & Stability

  • The update_client component has been improved to cancel pending background download tasks during shutdown, preventing potential issues or leaks. A new file utility for cleaning up directories was also added. (9d2e5ec)
  • As part of an ongoing code health effort, base/threading/platform_thread.h was updated to include its direct dependencies (<cstdint>, <string>, <vector>) following 'Include What You Use' (IWYU) principles. (ab87882)

Extensions

  • The internal schema for the contentScripts extension API has been converted from the legacy .idl format to the modern .webidl format. This is a code health and tooling improvement with no functional change to the public-facing API for extension developers. (c1640e1)

2026-02-27

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-02-27

Overview

This digest covers 715 commits on the main branch from commit (b004ff2) to (a0f393d), with 507 identified as relevant to developers on Chromium-based projects. The day saw an unusual number of breaking changes to core public APIs in base, content, net, and url, requiring code updates for embedders. Notable feature work includes the chrome.serial API removal from desktop platforms, the stabilization of the refreshed Task Manager UI, and continued improvements to Vertical Tabs.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public API function url::EncodeURIComponent has been renamed to url::EncodeUriComponent. Code directly calling this function must be updated. (6c112d6)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The method signatures for content::StoragePartition::ClearData and ClearDataForOrigin have changed, removing the quota_storage_remove_mask parameter. Embedders must update calls to these functions. (76d46af)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public class net::BackoffEntry is now marked final and its destructor is no longer virtual. Any code that subclasses net::BackoffEntry will fail to compile. (ff96c28)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The chrome.serial API has been removed from Windows, Mac, and Linux builds as part of the Chrome Apps deprecation. The API is now only available on ChromeOS. (f39e3b6)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public API base::trace_event::TraceLog::SetArgumentFilterPredicate() has been removed. Privacy filtering for trace arguments is now handled within the tracing service, and custom filtering via this method is no longer supported. (7287a60)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The method signature for views::WebView::TakeCrashedOverlayView has been changed to accept a callback for managing the overlay view's lifecycle. Code calling this method must be updated. (ff0a873)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING A static_assert has been added to the UmaHistogramEnumeration metric function, causing a compile-time failure if an enum's kMaxValue exceeds the maximum bucket count. Code using large enums with this function may now fail to build. (b717f55)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public static method LinearHistogram::FactoryGetWithRangeDescription() has been removed from base/metrics/histogram.h. Embedders must switch to an alternative factory method. (be046e7)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The header file extensions/browser/api/device_permissions_prompt.h has been renamed to extensions/browser/api/usb_device_permissions_prompt.h. Includes of the old path will fail. (1c660ca)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING In the network service, the public type aliases network::OriginatingProcess and network::RendererProcess have been removed. Code must be updated to use network::OriginatingProcessId and network::RendererProcessId. (9de6c1e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING A revert in the WebAudio API has removed the public method WebAudioBus::TryInitialize and related TryAllocate functions. Code that adopted these for non-crashing OOM handling will no longer compile. (3686b27)

APIs & Core Internals

  • A new SetFilter API was added to mojo::Remote<T>, allowing clients to inspect or modify outbound messages before they are sent. This mirrors existing functionality for incoming messages. (12ecb0a)
  • The internal "Legion" project has been renamed to "PrivateAI" across the codebase, and its crypto implementation was updated to use modern, stateless AEAD APIs. (2ceb181) (50e28f2) (f997bcc) (f5ce052)
  • The MemoryPressureListener in base/ now includes a sequence checker to enforce thread safety. (abf4312)

Extensions

  • The chrome.tabs.update() API now supports the highlighted property on desktop platforms, allowing extensions to programmatically add or remove a tab from the current selection. (f04cc6f)
  • The platform-agnostic TabsEventRouter will now handle the logic for tabs.onHighlighted and tabs.onHighlightChanged events, preparing for their implementation on other platforms. (f8bba03)
  • Foundational infrastructure was added for policy-driven filtering of extension activity logs, in preparation for enhanced enterprise telemetry. (4be68ed)

Browser & UI

  • The feature flag for the refreshed Task Manager UI has been removed, making it the default implementation. The old native macOS Task Manager code has been deleted. (5c16bf4) (9961485)
  • Significant work was done on the Vertical Tabs feature, including centralizing state management to resolve conflicts with immersive fullscreen mode and adding the ability to freeze the collapse state of tab groups. (b004ff2) (8f1266c) (7855d27)
  • The AutofillEnableVcn3dsAuthentication feature, which enables 3DS authentication for virtual card numbers, has been turned on by default. (e8f489a)
  • A new mechanism has been added to NavigationController::LoadURLParams to allow for "silent" scroll-to-text navigations, enabling features like cross-device scroll restoration without showing the standard visual highlight. (71f788d)
  • A new ui::ElementHighlighter helper has been introduced to generalize anchor highlights for ui::TrackedElements, which is now used by BubbleDialogDelegate. (c9fa491)

Security & Networking

  • The Device-Bound Sessions feature (kDeviceBoundSessions) is now enabled by default on Windows, removing the need for Origin Trial tokens. (067236d)
  • The fallback logic that attempted to verify SHA-1 signed certificates has been removed. Certificates using SHA-1 will now fail with a generic ERR_CERT_INVALID error. (180eeca)
  • A security fix was implemented to prevent the "thisisunsafe" keyword from bypassing enterprise block pages. (c09ea49)
  • A new public method, ClientSideDetectionHost::ReportUnsafeSite, was added to allow sending phishing reports that include screenshot data. This is paired with a new "Report Unsafe Site" menu item in the Help menu on macOS. (48ed90c) (5409480)
  • A bug fix in HostResolverManager::InvalidateCaches() addresses a potential crash by deferring the destruction of a probe's URLRequest to prevent re-entrant job modification. (dc86220)

Build & Code Health

  • Several modules were componentized to improve dependency management, including //chrome/browser/clipboard and //chrome/browser/collaboration/. (150bd48) (5f76bbc) (23278ae)
  • A bug in the Mojom C++ generator was fixed where forward-declaration headers could be generated without including <stdint.h>, even when types like uint32_t were used. (d4946a6)
  • Numerous calls to Vector::AppendVector() and Vector::AppendSpan() were migrated to the modern C++ append_range() method. (6b0ad3c) (b62e33f)

2026-02-26

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-02-26

Overview

A significant day with 682 commits, of which 492 were deemed relevant to developers building on Chromium. The day was marked by a large number of breaking API changes across the base, extensions, ui, and network components, requiring careful review by embedders. Changes spanned from core utility removals to significant refactoring of observer and network interfaces. The first relevant commit was (76aa3b0)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((76aa3b0)) and the last was (db2d44a)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((db2d44a)).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The TabListInterfaceObserver API was broken in multiple commits. A new virtual method OnHighlightedTabsChanged was added, and later, the signatures of all observer methods (e.g., OnTabAdded, OnTabRemoved) were changed to require a TabListInterface& as the first parameter. Implementations will fail to compile until updated. (6a0e2c0) (aa6b880) (8cdbdb6)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The base::VariantMap class has been completely removed from the codebase. All usages have been replaced with absl::flat_hash_map. Any project relying on base::VariantMap will break. (e3feeef)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The extensions::ContentHashReader class was removed from the public header extensions/browser/content_hash_reader.h and replaced with a free function. This is a breaking API change for code that directly used this class for extension content verification. (881021b)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Multiple public network and content APIs have had their signatures changed: - The Mojo interface network::mojom::NetworkContextClient::OnFileUploadRequested changed its process_id parameter from int to the strongly-typed ChildProcessId. - The function content::service_worker_client_utils::NavigateClient now requires the worker's security state to be passed for Local Network Access (LNA) checks. (837710a) (ffbeba9)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The remoting::SignalingMessage variant has been refactored to hold a JingleMessage instead of an XmlElement, breaking consumers of the remoting API who construct or inspect these messages. (ada63d3)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The legacy PwaInstallView class and its associated feature flag kPageActionsMigrationPwaInstall have been removed from chrome/browser/ui/views/page_action/, affecting embedders who may have used this UI view directly. (7f0d235)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The Compositor (cc) public API has been changed: - The PaintOpBuffer::DeepCopyAsRecord method signature in cc/paint/paint_op_buffer.h is now const, and its behavior was modified. - The public method drawElementImagePlaceholder has been removed from the cc::RecordPaintCanvas class. (d35f1ea) (4588ad6)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The PersistentCache API in components/services/storage/ has been updated. The key parameter for Find and Insert methods was changed from std::string_view to base::span<const uint8_t>, requiring callers to adapt to the new binary data type. (8a8c079)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The command-line switch kEnableBenchmarking has been moved from components/variations/variations_switches.h to base/base_switches.h. Code referencing the old path will fail to compile. (7a6bb4c)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The AccessibilityAnnotationSpecifics sync proto has been updated. Fields representing dates as int64 unix timestamps have been removed and replaced with a new NaiveDate message type, breaking components that directly serialize this sync entity. (0f43be9)

Core Internals & Tooling

  • The raw pointer overloads for base::File::Read and base::File::Write were briefly made private but the change was reverted due to build failures. Developers should be aware of the intent to migrate to base::span-based APIs. (ad342bc) (0c81119)
  • A new, more ergonomic one-shot AEAD encryption/decryption API was added to crypto/aead.h. (9ad1480)
  • The reentrancy policy for several observer classes, including RenderFrameObserver and DevToolsAgentHostObserver, was explicitly set to kAllowed to formalize existing behavior and prevent future crashes. (009262e) (58dc881)

Browser UI & Features

  • On macOS, Picture-in-Picture (PiP) windows will now correctly move to fullscreen spaces, improving the user experience. (af7cb90)
  • Support for interactive doodles on the New Tab Page has been removed. The underlying Mojo interfaces and handler logic have been deleted, simplifying the NTP implementation. (45ede36)
  • A use-after-free crash in MenuController was fixed by replacing a raw pointer with a ViewTracker, improving UI stability. (8edca3f)
  • A crash in Chrome for Testing and Headless Mode when running with --disable-infobars has been fixed. (e0f7f07)

Networking & Services

  • P2PSocketUdp on Windows was enhanced to retry sending packets after an ERR_NO_BUFFER_SPACE error, improving reliability for WebRTC. (be46c60)
  • The experimental SQL-based disk cache backend received new feature parameters (SqlDiskCacheReleaseMemoryAfterWrites, SqlDiskCacheCacheSize) to allow for fine-tuning of its SQLite configuration. (f8128f4)
  • The TcpConnectJob has been made more robust by ensuring it fully shuts down upon completion to prevent dangling pointers. (1d6f53a)

Extensions

  • The bluetoothPrivate API was converted from the legacy JSON schema format to the modern WebIDL format as part of ongoing modernization efforts. (48b52a5)
  • A new install prompt type, EXTENSION_PARENT_APPROVAL_PROMPT, was added for the supervised user extension installation flow. (5d0acf8)
  • The responsibility for persisting and restoring webRequest event listeners has been migrated to the central EventRouter, replacing a legacy ad-hoc implementation. (223feef)

Security

  • To prevent potential phishing, main-frame navigations to chrome://image are now blocked by a navigation throttle. This URL is intended only for subresource loading in WebUI. (ef26f22)
  • A new kill switch, kRevokeTokensWithInvalidCharactersOnLoad, was added to validate and clear invalid refresh tokens on load to prevent later crashes. (fad8b7e)
  • A crash in TokenBindingHelper was fixed that occurred when an empty binding key was passed to the crypto backend. The code now correctly handles this case. (6323f50)

Developer Tools

  • A new DevTools protocol method, Emulation.setPrimaryScreen, was added for headless automation, allowing tests to simulate different primary screen configurations. (2f1253d)
  • Cookie handling logic has been refactored to centralize permission checks into a new static method, NetworkHandler::CanAccessCookie, providing a unified and more secure way to manage cookie access for the Network and Storage domains. (9a6fcf2)

2026-02-25

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-02-25

Overview

This daily summary for the main branch on February 25, 2026, covers 719 commits, with 521 identified as relevant to developers working on Chromium-based projects. This update features a significant number of breaking changes to core base, ui, and remoting libraries that will require code updates for embedders. Other key changes include new extension APIs for Autofill, enhancements to the DevTools Protocol, and important fixes to networking and UI components. The first relevant commit is (a4822bc) and the last is (8692434).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The deprecated base/win/wincrypt_shim.h header has been removed. Projects on Windows must now include <windows.h> and <wincrypt.h> directly, which may require include order adjustments. (44a2896)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The remoting protocol library has removed the virtual method SignalStrategy::SendStanza and changed the signature of IPseudoTcpNotify::TcpWritePacket to use base::span. Embedders who implement these interfaces must update their code. (8e18de6) (6338506)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The constructor for base::MemoryConsumerRegistration and related registry methods now take an std::optional<MemoryConsumerTraits> instead of a direct object, requiring updates for all direct instantiations. (fad4543)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING In the core UI library, the public method ui::ColorVariant::IsSemantic() has been renamed to IsLogical(). Embedders using this method for custom UI components must update their code. (42552d9)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public function base::Histogram::InspectConstructionArguments in base/metrics/histogram.h now returns a ConstructionArgumentsValidity enum instead of void, breaking direct callers. (0bc602b)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The Web Printing API is no longer available to Chrome Platform Apps. This feature removal will break any remaining apps that relied on this functionality. (7c68e46)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The legacy OfferNotificationIconView and its feature flag kOfferNotificationPageActionIcon have been removed. Embedders explicitly using this UI component will be impacted. (c83d0bf)

APIs and Extensions

  • A new extension API, autofill.other_datatypes_enabled, has been added to the privacy settings. This allows extensions to control whether non-address and non-payment autofill data types are enabled. (4dba39f) (0de8cdc) (a64e731)
  • The DevTools Protocol has been enhanced. A new Extensions.listUnpackedExtensions command allows querying local unpacked extensions, and Emulation.setDeviceMetricsOverride now accepts a scrollbarType parameter. (bc7e885) (4471a6c)
  • A new method, GetAllTabsForProfile, has been added to the TabStripExperimentService API, allowing clients like Tab Search to fetch the tab hierarchy for all browser windows in a profile. (14274bc)
  • A bug in the extensions Web Request API has been fixed where event listeners for <webview> tags were incorrectly removed when a WebUI was moved between a tab and a side panel. (e91a60f)
  • In the experimental AI Language Model API, the onquotaoverflow event has been renamed to oncontextoverflow to more accurately reflect that it relates to the context window size. (6b82beb)

Browser and UI

  • On Windows, a new color ID kColorInfoBarButtonIconHovered has been introduced to fix a bug where the infobar close button icon would become invisible on hover when using custom dark themes. (51558c8)
  • Several Lens-related enterprise policies (e.g., LensCameraAssistedSearchEnabled) are now officially deprecated, with their functionality consolidated under the SearchContentSharing policy. (c711e48)
  • For enterprise reporting, a SaasUsageReportScheduler has been added to the ChromeBrowserCloudManagementController on desktop platforms to handle SaaS usage reporting. (b0ab60f)
  • The "flyover" animation for the Chrome Next UI is now enabled by default, changing the visual feedback for some UI interactions. (f413a5a)

Core Internals and Base Library

  • A new convenient overload has been added to DecodeUrlEscapeSequences in url/url_util.h that directly returns a decoded UTF-8 std::string. (f3aa498)
  • The session restore mechanism has been simplified by removing support for legacy session file paths used prior to Chrome version 85 (released in 2020). (c6f736a)
  • A critical crash in the sequence_manager has been fixed. The crash occurred when a task queue was unregistered while one of its tasks attempted to cancel another. (bc55c46)
  • The BrowserList class was refactored to remove its dependency on BrowserWindowInterface, an internal change preceding the eventual removal of BrowserList as part of the "bedrock" UI project. (df33d7f)

Networking and Security

  • On Windows, a WebRTC bug was fixed where ECN (Explicit Congestion Notification) options were not being set correctly on UDP sockets, which impacted L4S congestion control features. (f4d26f2)
  • The certificate verification process has been updated to support validity constraints based on a certificate's notBefore timestamp, allowing for more flexible, two-stage enforcement strategies. (15ed35d)
  • The connection allowlist is now enforced for link rel=preconnect to enhance security by preventing preconnections to origins not on the list. (1f13c15)

Testing and Infrastructure

  • A new TestingBrowserProcessDeathTestMixin has been introduced to properly initialize dependencies for death tests that rely on TestingBrowserProcess. (99798d1)
  • A MockActivationController class has been relanded for Windows and macOS to better emulate widget activation in tests, improving test reliability for UI components. (8a2462b)
  • The build system has been simplified by removing the disable_unknown_warning_option flag. Downstream projects are now expected to patch warnings locally. (9ba319a)

2026-02-13

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-02-13

Overview

This digest covers 100 commits from (16d6196)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((16d6196)) to (05cfd3b)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((05cfd3b)). After filtering for changes impacting browser-level development on desktop platforms, this summary highlights 4 major breaking changes and key updates across APIs, UI, Networking, and Code Health.

APIs & Browser Core

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: url/ library functions have been renamed to align with the Google C++ style guide (e.g., ConvertFromUTF16 is now ConvertFromUtf16). Embedders directly using these public functions must update their code. (cf08f47)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: A new virtual function, WillCreateURLLoaderFactoryForPrefetch, has been added to the public ContentBrowserClient interface. Embedders must implement this new function to compile. This change is part of an effort to add request integrity headers to prefetch requests. (50aaada)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public header chrome/browser/mac/bluetooth_utility.h and its implementation have been removed. While the commit states it was unused internally, any external project on macOS depending on this header will break. (0812c48)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: As part of a PWA sync refactoring, the public method WebApp::SetSyncProto() has been removed. Code that creates WebApp objects from sync data must now use a different approach, as the method for setting the underlying sync proto directly is gone. (bef7e56)
  • The StartWorkerForScope callback for Service Workers is now deferred until the OnStarted event has been processed. This ensures consumers (like extensions) will see the worker as "running" immediately after the callback, fixing a race condition. (a246d71)
  • The topK and temperature parameters in the Language Model API have been deprecated and are now restricted to extension contexts only. Use in regular web pages will trigger console warnings. (05cfd3b)

Browser UI & Features

  • An animation bug in Vertical Tabs has been fixed where the hovercard would animate from incorrect bounds when moving between pinned and unpinned tabs. (b15a283)
  • An experimental "Summarize" button, guarded by a feature flag, has been added to the PDF viewer toolbar. It is not yet functional. (f7fc174)
  • New feature flags and parameters have been added to allow for experimentation with the default browser prompt surface. (379689f)
  • A bug has been fixed in the PWA manifest update job where an 'old' icon could not be found if it only existed in fallback bitmap data, ensuring smoother icon updates. (ad42840)

Networking & Code Health

  • A crash in the SQL-based disk cache (net/disk_cache/sql) has been fixed. The crash occurred when Doom() was called on an entry that was still being created. (2c9fe70)
  • Multiple commits continue the effort to modernize the codebase, including replacing raw pointer-based HexEncode calls with std::span-based versions and simplifying the Windows clipboard ReadAsync template. (26be541) (cc74455) (54dbafc) (c83687b)
  • The remoting protocol implementation continues to be refactored to replace legacy XmlElement usage with well-defined structs, improving type safety and maintainability. (2230288) (8126d3b)

Testing & Infrastructure

  • Several flaky tests have been addressed. A CookieControls IPH test was disabled, and the Readability feature is now disabled in some automated tests to prevent flakiness related to DOM distillation. (d62916c) (15c5f14)
  • Browser tests for StartupBrowserCreator and BrowserNavigatorTest were updated to accommodate ongoing refactors and new features like the WebUI Omnibox popup. (6fca8f0) (2db83a4)

2026-02-12

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-02-12

Overview

This daily summary for February 12, 2026, covers 533 relevant commits out of a total of 708 on the main branch. The day was marked by an unusually high number of breaking changes affecting core APIs for UI, media, networking, and platform integration. Key themes include the removal of deprecated code, modernization of interfaces using factories and strongly-typed enums, and significant behavioral shifts in sign-in and browsing history. The first and last commits analyzed are (8826042) and (ff12dc7).

API & Core Interfaces

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public method BrowserWindow::IsBorderlessModeEnabled() has been renamed to IsUnframedModeEnabled(). Embedders must update their calls to check the window state for Web Apps. (f10b658)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public remoting::protocol::Authenticator interface was refactored. Methods ProcessMessage and GetNextMessage now use a JingleAuthentication struct instead of a raw jingle_xmpp::XmlElement*, requiring significant updates for protocol implementers. (ccb7f74)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: Deprecated constructors using ChannelLayout were removed from media::AudioDecoderConfig. Embedders must migrate to the constructors that accept ChannelLayoutConfig. (ab38239)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The virtual method OnTabBlockedStateChange has been removed from the public TabStripModelObserver interface. Subclasses overriding this method will fail to compile. (73da240)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The signature of WebViewGuest::LoadAbort was changed. Its error_code parameter is now a net::Error enum instead of an int. Embedders of the <webview> tag must update their handlers. (e1a3f28)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The BrowsingHistoryService now groups similar visits (same hostname and title) on a per-day basis. This is a significant behavioral change that will alter the results of history queries. (f327600)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: Several public headers and classes were removed, including components/prefs/transparent_unordered_string_map.h, the Windows-specific device::DeviceInfoQueryWin class, and the legacy V1 crypto/apple/keychain.h API for macOS. (162ca81) (df447bf) (e338af7)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The legion::SecureChannel interface was refactored to use a Factory pattern, removing the public EstablishChannel and SetResponseCallback methods and changing the initialization flow. (2eb439d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The deprecated Get() method was removed from the blink::mojom::StorageArea mojom interface. Consumers of this IPC interface must now use GetAll() to retrieve storage data. (ca61807)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The 'Read Aloud' accessibility feature is now enabled by default, and the public function IsReadAnythingReadAloudEnabled() has been removed from ui/accessibility/accessibility_features.h. (8771a1c)
  • A new OnErrorStateChanged method was added to the PasswordStoreInterface::Observer, which will allow clients to be notified of error state changes in the password store in the future. (bd310a2)

Sign-in & Identity

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The DICE sign-in migration is now forced. Feature flags that allowed embedders to control this flow (e.g., kOfferMigrationToDiceUsers) have been removed, eliminating external control over this UI behavior. (1581e94)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The signin_metrics::AccessPoint::kUnknown enum value has been removed. Code using this for sign-in metrics will fail to compile and must be updated to use a more specific access point. (b3acbdc)

Security & Policy

  • Foundational work for launching an isolated browser process on Windows has been implemented, including a new command-line switch and a basic COM interface. (0b67c8c) (5655a04)
  • Security was enhanced by adding validation in RenderFrameHostImpl::CreateNewWindow to terminate renderers that send an invalid WindowOpenDisposition, preventing a potential browser process crash. (4d0b870)
  • A new enterprise policy, ForceForegroundPriorityForAllTabs, has been implemented to force all tabs to run at foreground priority. It is managed via local state and is not dynamically refreshable. (137c7e0)

Browser UI & Web Apps

  • To support upcoming Web App migrations, a new HasPendingMigration() method was added to AppBrowserController. This is used to display a 'Review update' menu item and toolbar chip to the user. (5005124)
  • The DOM Storage backend now supports value compression using ZSTD or Snappy, an internal change aimed at reducing the disk space used by localStorage and sessionStorage. (50fe35a)
  • The Privacy Sandbox notice dialog and its associated WebUI resources (chrome://privacy-sandbox-dialog) have been removed from the browser. (39a014e)

Extensions

  • The ProxyOverrideRules private API was enabled by default, and the autofill_private API now provides more fine-grained error messages to aid in debugging. (d49f889) (2168b2c)
  • A warning will now be logged during extension installation if the version string in the manifest is parsable but does not serialize back to the same string, helping developers catch formatting inconsistencies. (121ff9a)

Networking

  • Documentation for NetworkChangeNotifier was updated to warn developers that its connection status can be unreliable and should not be used to block all network requests. (3355067)
  • The net::HostResolver and net::MappedHostResolver classes were improved to better support HostPortPair types in service endpoint requests, increasing flexibility for DNS lookups. (c68df85)

Testing

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: In the network testing utility net::FakeServiceEndpointResolution, the public method set_start_result() has been removed. Downstream tests must migrate to CompleteStartSynchronously() to configure mock DNS resolution. (21d7e84)

2026-02-11

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-02-11

Overview

A summary of 551 relevant commits out of 731 total from February 11, 2026, focusing on changes that impact developers working with Chromium. This digest highlights numerous breaking API changes, along with significant updates to core browser APIs, networking, security, extensions, and UI components. View the full range of commits from (64691e8) to (5bd6aa3).

Core & APIs

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The signature for EnumTraits::FromMojom in mojo/public/cpp/bindings/enum_traits.h has been changed to support more idiomatic C++ return types. The legacy bool FromMojom(MojomType, UserType*) pattern is replaced by std::optional<UserType> FromMojom(MojomType). Custom EnumTraits specializations must be updated. (beab4f6)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The given_name and full_name members of components::signin::public::identity_manager::AccountInfo are now private. Embedders must switch to using the GetGivenName() and GetFullName() accessor methods. (79c150b)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The history.mojom interface and components::history::core::browser::HistoryEntry class have changed. The all_timestamps field is no longer a vector of timestamps but a map of URLs to timestamps, breaking any code that directly constructs or accesses this data. (15784bc)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The concrete class TranslationDispatcher has been refactored into an abstract base class. Embedders who were directly instantiating this class must now create an instance of a concrete implementation, such as GoogleApiTranslationDispatcher. (ca406ec)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The ChannelLayoutConfig class has been moved from media/base/audio_parameters.h to media/base/channel_layout.h. Includes must be updated. The class also now enforces stricter constraints, which may cause crashes if invalid channel counts are used. (103fb04)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public method CommandStorageManager::Init() has been removed from the components/sessions service as part of a cleanup. (03d0b09)
  • A new lease-based system for managing thread priorities has been introduced in base::PlatformThread. PlatformThread::RaiseThreadTypeLease is now the preferred RAII-based mechanism for performance tuning over the older SetCurrentThreadType. (612357b)

Networking & Security

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The RestrictedCookieManager::SetCanonicalCookie mojom API signature has changed. It no longer accepts a net::CanonicalCookie object and instead requires a RestrictedCanonicalCookieParamsPtr struct, moving cookie creation responsibility to the browser process. (c91ac9d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public method SiteInstance::IsSandboxed() has been removed. Code must be updated to use site_instance->GetSecurityPrincipal().IsSandboxed() to access this information. (6d9a124)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The API for FederatedActorLoginRequest has been modified. Consumers must now call the new public method OnFederatedResultReceived(result) instead of manually invoking a callback. (8c9e031)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The signature for RenderFrameHostDelegate::UpdateTitle and WebContentsImpl::UpdateTitle has changed, removing the base::i18n::TextDirection parameter. (df35cb4)
  • A security vulnerability in the Payment Request API that allowed probing of localhost has been patched. (32a1e33)

Extensions

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The extensions::Message class has been refactored to support Blob types, changing its internal data representation. Code that directly accessed the message's data buffer will break. (d9bea47)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public class DevicePermissionsPrompt has been renamed to UsbDevicePermissionsPrompt and its virtual methods have been removed. Embedders using or extending this UI for device permissions must update their code. (d345c49)
  • A new mechanism ensures an extension's service worker is started at least once per version to reliably register its event listeners, fixing a potential initialization bug. (5b375a5)

Browser & UI

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public method TabStripModel::SetOpenerOfWebContentsAt has been removed. Code must be updated to use the new TabStripModel::SetOpenerOfTabAt(int, tabs::TabInterface*) method signature. (023d799)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The signature of TabListInterface::DiscardTab has changed. The method now returns content::WebContents* instead of void. All implementations of this interface must be updated. (fe82665)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public enum value blink::mojom::DisplayMode::kBorderless has been renamed to kUnframed. Code referencing the old name will fail to compile. (3a77577)
  • Bubble dialogs can now reposition correctly when their anchor view moves. This is enabled by the new OnViewVisibleBoundsChanged method in the views::ViewObserver interface. (a77ad4b)
  • The ui::Layer class can now automatically compute bounds for backdrop filters (e.g., blur), simplifying the implementation of effects like blurred backgrounds. (e73ede1)

Testing & Build System

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public test utility function content::HasSandboxedSiteInstance has been removed from content/public/test/browser_test_utils.h. Tests must be updated to check the security principal directly. (854324a)
  • A new GN argument, experimental_linker_path, has been added to facilitate testing with alternative linkers like mold. (3529dcf)
  • The official C++ style guide was updated to formally ban several previously undecided C++20 features. (f61c419)

2026-02-10

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-02-10

Overview

On February 10, 2026, the Chromium main branch saw 759 commits, with 590 being relevant to developers on Windows and macOS. This day was marked by a significant number of breaking API changes across core components like UI, Identity, Media, and Extensions, requiring embedders to perform code updates. The first relevant commit was (8935f34) and the last was (77cf016).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING Core: The public API method GURL::ComponentStringPiece() has been renamed to GURL::ComponentStringView() in url/gurl.h, requiring embedders to update all call sites. (caf65ac)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING UI: The public ui::Clipboard API signature has changed. Asynchronous read methods like ReadText now accept const std::optional<DataTransferEndpoint>& instead of a raw pointer for the destination endpoint. (d704671)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING UI: The TabStripModelObserver interface has been marked as re-entrant. This changes the notification contract and may break embedder logic that assumes non-re-entrant notifications. (9e81acd)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING UI: The mechanism for retrieving TabListInterface from BrowserWindowInterface has changed. Embedders must now request the base interface via UserData instead of a concrete implementation class. (c427eed)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Identity: Multiple sign-in APIs were changed. PrimaryAccountMutator::SetPrimaryAccount() now requires an explicit access_point, and OAuth2ScopeRestriction::kExplicitConsent was removed, breaking AccessTokenFetcher constructors that used it. (70eb35f) (d7489b8)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Identity: The FedCM IdentityDialogController::OnFlowCompleted method signature was changed from taking a bool to content::webid::FederatedLoginResult to provide more granular results. (702561c)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Payments: The public enum AutofillProgressDialogType was renamed to AutofillProgressUiType, which also changes the signature of methods in PaymentsAutofillClient that use it. (5830fae)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Media: The public method media::VideoFrame::IsMappable() has been renamed to HasDirectCpuAccess() to improve clarity. All calls must be updated. (8465918)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Extensions: The extensions::Message type is now move-only. Public methods in the messaging system like MessagePort::DispatchOnMessage now take Message by value, requiring call sites to use std::move. (6f16df7)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Extensions: The public API method DevicePermissionsPrompt::AskForHidDevices() has been removed from the extensions framework, as the corresponding chrome.hid API was previously removed. (dbb6168)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Networking: P2P/WebRTC code has been decoupled into a new network_cpp_p2p component. Embedders using this functionality must now add a build dependency on //services/network/public/cpp:cpp_p2p. (e7e9679)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Windows Sandbox: The IPCInt class and CountedBuffer class alias have been removed from the public header sandbox/win/src/internal_types.h, breaking custom sandbox policy implementations. (d4f4ac3)

APIs and Extensions

  • A new public interface, SecurityPrincipal, has been introduced in content/public/browser to represent security contexts, replacing the internal SiteInfo class. A corresponding GetSecurityPrincipal() method was added to SiteInstance. (5632420)
  • A new controller has been added to manage user confirmation when an extension controls the default search engine, triggering a dialog for explicit user consent. (aa15000)
  • A race condition was fixed that could cause a crash when accessing a Chrome Extension asset while the browser is shutting down. (d433d8b)
  • A new private extension API, proxyOverrideRulesPrivate, was added to allow management of ProxyOverrideRules preferences. (1a0ce6a)

Browser UI and Features

  • On macOS, NativeWidgetMac::IsVisibleOnAllWorkspaces is now fully implemented, correctly synchronizing the 'visible on all workspaces' state between the browser and remote App Shim windows. (12f1d11)
  • A hang in the Share Menu on macOS was fixed by deferring the expensive population of sharing services until the menu is actually opened. (1708b56)
  • A new DefaultBrowserDialogManager was introduced to handle showing the 'set as default browser' dialog across all windows on applicable platforms. (95b70d9)
  • The experimental Vertical Tabs feature received multiple updates, including a crash fix when enabling TouchUI, UI improvements for drag-and-drop, and a new algorithm for handling clicks in the toolbar area. (b4ecafc) (82668c3) (37fc570)
  • A new policy, IsolatedWebAppUserInstallationEnabled, was introduced to allow administrators to control whether users can manually install Isolated Web Apps. (b0033ca)

Security and Performance

  • On Windows, Process::Terminate(exit_code, wait=false) was updated to be truly non-blocking by skipping a fallback wait when TerminateProcess() fails. (b1f58e7)
  • The VisitedLinkWriter now uses base::CheckedNumeric to calculate shared memory region size, mitigating potential integer overflow vulnerabilities. (da6930a)
  • WebAuthn will now show a more specific error message when a user attempts a create() operation but Google Password Manager passkeys are disabled by policy or settings. (ac6abd7)
  • The web_data_service_factory.cc has been modularized out of //chrome/browser into a new //chrome/browser/webdata_services target to reduce dependencies and improve build structure. (1606f27)

Core Internals and Media

  • Support was added for Dolby Vision profiles 10 (AV1-based) and 20 (MV-HEVC for 3D video), expanding video codec capabilities. (838c1e7)
