  • The AudioDecoderConfig in media/base now supports a ChannelLayoutConfig object through a new constructor and initialization overload. (77cf016)
  • The Page.getAnnotatedPageContent CDP command is now implemented in headless mode. (d3de7f3)

Testing and Infrastructure

  • A new HistoryEmbeddingSearch test interface was defined for Omnibox unit tests, allowing a fake implementation to be provided without pulling in all dependencies of HistoryEmbeddingsService. (397ddce)
  • The TabListInterface has been moved from //chrome/browser/ui/tabs to a more general location at //chrome/browser/tab_list to better reflect its use across desktop and mobile. (69560d1)
  • A fix was implemented in MessagePumpCFRunLoopBase on macOS to prevent a DCHECK failure caused by nested run loops during idle work, improving test stability. (99d3792)
  • Numerous feature flags for shipped features were removed, cleaning up the codebase. Removals include kPrefetchAsyncCancelOnCookiesChange, EarlyFullScreenVideoOptimization, and kAccessibilitySwitchAccessV3. (d0b9691) (f4c43ee) (06ed687)

2026-02-09

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-02-09

Overview

This summary covers 615 commits on the main branch from February 9th, 2026, with a focus on 451 changes relevant to developers building on Chromium. The day was marked by numerous breaking API changes across UI, networking, security, and core components, requiring code updates for embedders. The first relevant commit was (bcb84ec) and the last was (97b3f24).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING API Removal: The public virtual method ViewAccessibility::OverrideFocus has been removed from ui/views. Embedders must migrate to the new aria-activedescendant pattern by using ViewAccessibility::SetActiveDescendant. (30d8cde)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING API Removal: The public methods BrowserList::CloseAllBrowsersWithProfile() and BrowserList::CloseAllBrowsersWithIncognitoProfile() have been removed. Embedders must implement alternative logic for closing browsers associated with a specific profile. (d29ce19)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING API Removal: The public virtual method WindowController::CanClose() has been removed from the Extensions API. Embedders must now implement this check directly. (6c171b9)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING API Signature Change: The AutofillClient interface now includes a new pure virtual method, ShowAutofillAiFailureNotification. Embedders with custom AutofillClient implementations must add this method to avoid build failures. (095b4dd)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING API Signature Change: The ui::Clipboard methods ExtractCustomPlatformNames and ReadAvailableStandardAndCustomFormatNames are now asynchronous and require a callback to receive results. (63af498)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING API Signature Change: The DnsTransactionFactory::CreateTransaction method signature has changed. The bool secure parameter is replaced by a new AttemptMode enum to specify DNS or DNS-over-HTTPS. (17be678)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING API Signature Change: The UnexportableKeyService methods DeleteAllKeys and DeleteAllKeysSlowlyAsync (and their Mojo equivalents) no longer accept a priority parameter. (efa2d3b)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING API Signature Change: The PasswordChangeServiceInterface::IsPasswordChangeSupported method now accepts a const password_manager::PasswordForm& instead of a const GURL&. (4deeba3)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING API Signature Change: The core allocator setup function base::allocator::PartitionAllocSupport::ReconfigureAfterFeatureListInit now accepts a FeatureListConfiguration struct instead of multiple boolean parameters. (14484c4)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Component & API Change: The ResumableUploadRequest class has moved to components/enterprise/connectors/core and its constructor/factory methods now require a scoped_refptr<base::SequencedTaskRunner>, breaking existing instantiation patterns. (72adb91)

Browser UI and Core

  • A new menu item, "Report an unsafe site," has been added to the main application menu, with command handling in BrowserCommandController. (f5b26be)
  • The User Education system now prevents feature promos from appearing while an automated "Agent" is actively controlling a tab. (dd23914)
  • The Tab Search data model is now prevented from updating while it is being preloaded but not visible, improving performance. (43d2930)
  • The First Run Experience (FRE) features a refreshed design for the "set as default browser" screen. (ce66f9f)
  • A bug was fixed in the side panel toolbar that caused it to persist incorrectly after switching modes due to a use-after-move. (e8a9b02)

Security and Privacy

  • A new SearchIntegrity component has been introduced to monitor search engine configurations against an allowlist of trusted providers. (bf13717)
  • A new policy, WebRtcDiagnosticLogCollectionAllowedForOrigins, has been added to allow enterprise control over the WebRTC diagnostic logging API. (22400f0)
  • For improved privacy, the mechanism for tracking deleted profiles now uses relative basenames instead of full, absolute paths to avoid leaking system information. (95b8d19)
  • The PermissionServiceImpl now validates that incoming permission requests are not empty, hardening the service against malformed requests from a renderer process. (3258f06)

Networking

  • The RenderDocument feature and its SkipRendererCancellationThrottle optimization have been enabled by default, which may alter navigation cancellation behavior for improved performance. (b4d0474)
  • The rollout of NetworkServicePerPriorityTaskQueues was reverted due to a suspected bug. (03e9c1a)

Base, Performance, and Core Utilities

  • On Windows and macOS, the memory suppression features kSuppressMemoryListeners and kSuppressMemoryMonitor are now enabled by default to reduce memory pressure notifications. (bed11e0)
  • On macOS, file quarantine logic in base/mac/mac_util.mm now uses the official NSURLQuarantinePropertiesKey API instead of a private extended attribute. (0b330d2)
  • A new experimental CPU Performance API has been implemented to provide performance information based on CPU manufacturer, core count, and model. This is not yet exposed to web content. (1b46924)

Testing and Infrastructure

  • The base::test::TestFuture utility has been enhanced with a kQueue mode, allowing it to queue multiple values for testing scenarios with multiple asynchronous callbacks. (0223ed4)
  • An issue where DevTools clients would fail to interact with unloaded tabs has been addressed by forcing the WebContents to render when a client auto-attaches. (216858a)
  • The Chromium Updater will now upload its history log in crash reports to aid in debugging client-side update issues. (ad48b67)
  • The signin_util in end-to-end tests was updated to support signing into the browser without enabling full sync, improving test scenarios for features like supervised users. (3efcdcf)

2026-02-08

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-02-08

Overview

This digest covers 13 commits, filtering out changes specific to Android, iOS, ChromeOS, and the Blink rendering engine to focus on updates relevant to desktop embedders. The analysis spans from commit (6f28564) to (9d99dc0), with no major breaking changes identified in stable public APIs.

Core UI Framework (Views)

  • The internal observer lists for views::View and views::Widget have been updated to explicitly allow re-entrant notifications. This change modifies the internal base::ObserverList implementation to prevent crashes in specific re-entrancy scenarios but does not alter the public API signatures for AddObserver or RemoveObserver. (bef2886) (68c48f5)

Browser Features

  • The experimental contextual_tasks feature received updates. A new feature flag was added to disable the auto-suggestion chip, and the dismissal behavior for this chip is now scoped per-thread and per-URL, rather than for the entire browser session. (d18f07b) (fa4869f)
  • A minor typo in the conditional rendering logic for the managed user profile notice UI has been corrected. (214bea7)

Testing

  • Several browser tests have been made more robust to prevent failures caused by unrelated UI elements. FrameNodeImplBrowserTest and PageLoadTrackerDecoratorTest now filter nodes to only those relevant to the test's WebContents. ProcessManagementBrowserTest was updated to use dynamic process limits instead of hardcoded values, making it more resilient to variations in startup processes. (9e156c4) (c86dedd) (022722b)

Localization

  • A routine update to translation files (.xtb) has been committed based on the latest strings from .grd files. (9d99dc0)

2026-02-07

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-02-07

Overview

This digest covers 100 commits from (7529914)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((7529914)) to (7553235)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((7553235)). After filtering for changes relevant to Windows and macOS browser development, this summary highlights key updates in Security, Browser UI, Extensions, Networking, and Web Applications. One major breaking change was identified, disabling Widevine hardware-secure codecs on Windows, which may impact protected media playback for some services.

Security & Privacy

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: Widevine hardware-secure decryption (HW_SECURE_ALL) has been disabled by default on Windows. This is a significant behavioral change to the stable Encrypted Media Extensions (EME) API. Services that rely on this specific robustness level for content protection will need to adapt, as requestMediaKeySystemAccess will no longer grant access for it. This change is part of a shift towards using PlayReady SL3000 for hardware DRM on Windows. (ea1b3b8)
  • Several internal security enhancements were made, including removing unsafe buffer usage in the Windows sandbox policy, ensuring the ChildProcessSecurityPolicy's SecurityState is kept alive during asynchronous file system path checks to prevent crashes, and refactoring the setup of Secure DNS preferences. (28ae1cc) (5698ea6) (29918fb)

Browser UI & Features

  • The 'Start Chrome with Windows' feature received several updates, including the wiring up of its opt-in/out infobar, UI updates to the settings toggle button, and new localizable strings. (58bd717) (c7683f5) (3ac9521)
  • The behavior of startup prompts for setting the default browser and pinning to the taskbar has been decoupled. An experiment will now show one or the other, but not a combined prompt, to better evaluate user engagement with each flow. (a8bbe5f)
  • A new 'Back to opener' item has been integrated into the back/forward navigation menu, allowing users to quickly return to the tab that opened the current one. This involved refactoring BackForwardMenuModel to support the new entry. (d5b9c16)
  • The Vertical Tabs feature saw several bug fixes and reversions to address crashes related to tab closing and dragging, while also updating separator padding. A change to render hovercards for vertical tabs was reverted due to a dangling pointer crash. (7629ff8) (956ab52) (7f161fd) (8ecc009)
  • A new WebUI surface chrome://feedback/report-unsafe-site has been added to provide a user-facing dialog for reporting unsafe sites. (2d0a217)
  • On macOS, the main menu bar's 'Tab' menu text will now update dynamically based on whether the user is in Right-to-Left (RTL) mode or has Vertical Tabs enabled. (8a3a16d) (f16cdcb)

Extensions

  • The offscreen.createDocument and offscreen.closeDocument API callbacks are now optional, improving developer ergonomics for contexts that do not use Promises. (dca5915)

Networking & Cache

  • The SQL disk cache backend has been significantly optimized. Changes include implementing lazy entry creation to avoid immediate database writes, deferring the actual insertion until data is written or the entry is closed. This reduces unnecessary database transactions for short-lived cache entries. (3ef364b) (2288d17) (a3b60c0)
  • The prefetching service underwent internal refactoring to clarify dependencies and improve code structure, such as moving the creation of the isolated URLLoaderFactory. (3c44de9) (aa792f6)

Omnibox

  • The internal protocol for configuring the Omnibox AIM (Advanced Intelligence in the Omnibox) feature has been updated. This includes adding new tool modes like kGeminiPro and kAimGenPrompt, and adding support for aim_url_params to ToolConfig and ModelConfig to allow for more dynamic URL generation. (435974d)
  • The SearchProvider will now restrict suggestions to only verbatim matches when in specific experimental tool modes, such as 'typed nano banana' or 'deep search'. (bbf2654)

Web Applications (PWAs)

  • The Web Install API, which powers the experimental <install> HTML element, has updated its UMA/UKM metrics to differentiate between different API versions and entry points. (72cea34)
  • Internal logic for finalizing a PWA installation and handling the PWA update dialog has been refactored for better code structure, moving logic into dedicated jobs and controllers. (788ce5c) (1aabde1)

PDF

  • The 'Save to Drive' feature for PDFs is now enabled on ChromeOS builds and is controllable via the RestrictPdfSaveToGoogleDriveAccountsToPattern enterprise policy. (10dada4)
  • A PDFium roll includes the removal of the internal CFX_FontMgr::NewFixedFace() function. Additionally, a fix was added to prevent a nullptr crash in the Windows PDF font mapper. (6841251) (25f63e8)

Developer Utilities & Internals

  • The ELF reader in base/debug has been updated to support larger BuildIDs, which is necessary for compatibility with newer linkers like wild that use larger hashes. (383ff55)
  • The ObserverLists for WindowTreeHost and FocusController have been explicitly marked to allow re-entrancy, preventing potential issues during complex event notifications like a window being destroyed during an activation change. (0596395) (f009f4d)

2026-02-06

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-02-06

Overview

This digest summarizes 689 commits to the Chromium main branch on February 6th, 2026, from which 539 were identified as relevant to developers of Chromium-based projects. The changes span from the first commit (5f62b6d) to the last (265741b). This summary highlights multiple breaking changes to core browser interfaces, networking APIs, and metrics services, requiring developer action.

API & Core Interfaces

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The public LocationBar interface has been updated with several new pure virtual methods, including GetBrowser() and IsDrawn(). Any custom implementation of this core UI interface must be updated to avoid build failures. (7665950)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The public AccountInfo struct no longer allows direct member access for full_name and given_name. Code must be updated to use the new accessor methods GetFullName() and GetGivenName(). (623d7f8)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: Several public virtual methods have been removed from WindowController, including HasEditableTabStrip(), SupportsTabs(), and IsDeleteScheduled(). Callers must migrate to using helpers on TabListInterface or ExtensionTabUtil. (f706a1b) (8cc423f) (46847f4)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The skills::SkillsService public API has been modified. The AddOrUpdateSkillFromSync pure virtual function now requires a new description parameter, and the IsInitialized() method has been replaced with an enum-based GetServiceStatus() method. (b34d141) (c3d3652)
  • The MenuModel API now supports adding a "Preview" badge to menu items, in addition to the existing "New" badge, allowing for more descriptive UI hints. (e2dcab8)
  • A new virtual method, ConfirmInfoBarDelegate::GetButtonStyle(), was added to allow for custom button styling in ConfirmInfoBar, providing more UI control for embedders. (e0195fc)

Browser UI & Extensions

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The public utility function ExtensionTabUtil::GetEditableTabStripModel() has been removed. Embedders must update their code to get the TabStripModel directly from the Browser object. (854164e)
  • The window.setResizable(bool) API is now supported for standalone windowed PWAs that use tabs. Previously, this was restricted to tab-less PWAs. (c19b719)
  • A new OnAllTabsAreClosing method was added to the TabListInterfaceObserver to notify clients when a mass tab closure event begins, allowing for more timely reactions. (3649dd0)
  • The video Picture-in-Picture window now supports seeking forward and backward using the left and right arrow keys. (eba95f7)
  • On Windows, OS shortcut management for PWAs has been improved to filter by app_id, fixing bugs where shortcuts for a new app could be incorrectly deleted during app migrations. (aa4aa5e)

Networking & Security

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: All instances of "Private Network Access" (PNA) have been renamed to "Local Network Access" (LNA). This affects numerous public mojom interfaces (e.g., network::mojom::DevToolsObserver), classes, and constants, requiring significant updates for any code interacting with these network service APIs. (d28a1ec)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: On macOS, a major refactor of keychain access within os_crypt has removed the public OSCrypt::SetKeychainForTesting method. Test setups must be migrated to use the OSCryptMocker utility instead. (158a2d2)
  • The GetToken endpoint will now include the Sec-CH-UA-Platform header, allowing the authentication server to make policy decisions based on the client's operating system. (2ed91e2)
  • Isolated Web Apps (IWA) key management now allows for "soft" key rotations using a previous_key field, preventing the app from appearing broken during an update cycle. (8d36a5d) (2b72489)

Autofill & Payments

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The AutofillManager::Observer::OnFieldTypesDetermined method signature has changed. Implementations must add the new bool small_forms_were_parsed parameter to their method definition. (524d57f)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The public constant chrome::kYourSavedInfoSubPage has been removed and the function chrome::ShowYourSavedInfo has been renamed to chrome::ShowAutofill. Code navigating to this settings page must be updated. (0629950)
  • Internal Wallet/Payments APIs for UpsertPublicPass and UpsertPrivatePass have been migrated from JSON to Protobuf for network requests to improve efficiency and type safety. (f37b98b) (3b7648b) (883ab96)

Performance & Metrics

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The Structured Metrics service has undergone a major threading refactor. StructuredMetricsRecorder is no longer ref-counted (now uses std::unique_ptr), and the synchronous EventStorage::TakeEvents method is now asynchronous with a callback. Embedders using this service must update their implementation. (8f66e3b)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The page_load_metrics.mojom interface has been altered by a large refactoring of Interaction to Next Paint (INP) metrics. This changes how EventTiming data is passed and will break consumers of this performance metrics API. (f5cd024)
  • The first set of features for the "PerfCombined2025" initiative has been enabled for Windows and Mac, including kPageAllocatorRetryOnCommitFailure and kAbslFlatMapInVariantMap, aimed at improving memory allocation and container efficiency. (17a08b0)
  • The thread pool's internal task sorting key now uses ThreadType instead of TaskPriority to represent importance. This is a foundational change for future task scheduling improvements. (2ece136) (3a96ae1)

Build & Code Health

  • A new command has been added to the DevTools UI bindings, allowing DevTools to trigger a browser restart. This is intended to support certain experiments that require a restart to take effect. (41808e6)
  • As part of an ongoing code health effort, several internal uses of std::unordered_map in components like PrefChangeRegistrar have been replaced with the more performant absl::flat_hash_map. (ea8759d) (355ee25) (138ca23)

2026-02-05

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-02-05

Overview

This summary covers 723 commits on the main branch, from commit (ffcc9cb) to (483644a). After filtering for changes relevant to developers on Windows and macOS, 556 commits were analyzed. This digest highlights multiple significant breaking changes to core APIs, along with improvements to browser features, networking, security, and testing infrastructure.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The base::File API has been updated. The Read() and Write() methods now require base::span instead of raw pointers and size arguments. All code performing file I/O with base::File must be updated to use the new span-based signatures. (dd02263)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The widely used testing utility ui_test_utils::WaitForBrowserToClose() has been removed. This function was prone to hangs. Test suites must be migrated to use the more robust ui_test_utils::BrowserDestroyedObserver to await browser closure. (fef9f36) (1eeffe6) (11ba8dd)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The FeatureVariation struct in components/webui/flags/feature_entry.h now uses base::span. Embedders who define their own feature flags via this public API must update their definitions to match the new struct layout. (2e89d3b)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING In the gin V8 helper library, gin::ArrayBuffer and gin::ArrayBufferView have been modified. The data() and num_bytes() methods were removed in favor of span-based getters (bytes() and mutable_bytes()), and a constructor overload taking v8::Isolate* was also removed. (e490adb) (d570054)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Public C-style enums in ui/touch_selection have been converted to enum class. Code using these enums must be updated to use scoped names, e.g., ui::TouchSelectionController::ActiveStatus::kInactive. (e1c7925)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING GLES 3.1 function bindings have been removed from ui/gl and gpu/. Embedders relying on these graphics bindings will experience build failures. (9fc655a)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public URL parsing functions DoesContainWindowsDriveSpecUntil() and DoesBeginWindowsDriveSpec() have changed their return contract. The "not found" value is now string_view::npos instead of -1. (20e24b0)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING In the graphics stack, ui::gfx::NativePixmap::GetBufferFormatModifier() was renamed to GetFormatModifier(), and the public utility viz::HasEquivalentBufferFormat() was removed entirely. (64a8baa) (c75850d)

Core APIs and Internals

  • The [[nodiscard]] attribute has been added to WebContents::StartPrefetch and BrowserContext::StartPrefetch. This will generate a compiler warning if the returned PrefetchHandle is not used, helping to prevent resource leaks. (63a6759)
  • The TabRestoreService API now includes a RemoveLeastRecentlyUsedEntries method, allowing for efficient bulk removal of session entries with a single UI update. (ba69fd5)
  • The WebAppFilter class now supports bitwise & and | operators, enabling the creation of complex composite filters from simple ones (e.g., IsIsolatedApp() | IsIsolatedSubApp()). (10076c5)
  • The StickyActivationManager is now thread-safe, ensuring that updates to preferences from field trial variations are always posted to the correct sequence to prevent race conditions. (d851b1d)
  • The OpenTabsUIDelegate interface has a new method, GetForeignSessionTimestamps(), to provide more accurate device activity timestamps for the "Send Tab to Self" feature. (e0a4cfa)
  • As part of an ongoing code health effort, many instances of std::unordered_map and std::set have been replaced with the more performant absl::flat_hash_map and absl::flat_hash_set across the codebase. (4d85445) (fa5d26b) (d26ac35) (128090b)

Networking and Security

  • On Windows, the browser can now monitor the OS certificate store for changes in real-time. This enables automatic synchronization of the trust store without requiring a browser restart, a significant improvement for enterprise environments. (515c9bd)
  • Safe Browsing's Client-Side Detection (CSD) will now perform semantic analysis on clipboard content to detect and report commands that may download and execute remote scripts, enhancing protection against clipboard-based attacks. (33374cd)
  • The disk cache backend has been refactored to use a CacheEntryHasher interface. This allows a custom hasher to be used with encryption, preventing URL discovery by checking against URL hash tables. (9e5a48e)
  • The SQL disk cache's write buffering logic has been improved to better handle large data streams and more accurately track memory usage, leading to more efficient I/O and preventing excessive memory consumption. (6048204) (767da05)
  • Foundational plumbing was added to URLLoader to allow clients to provide a pre-configured data pipe for a response body. This is a non-breaking addition that will enable future "zero-copy" response optimizations. (da594d0)
  • The migration to asynchronous OS-level encryption for Sync data is complete, and the kSyncUseOsCryptAsync feature flag has been removed. (f1aed8b) (a741396)

Browser UI and Features

  • A fix has been implemented on macOS to allow drag-and-drop of downloads from PWA windows. A new Mojo interface correctly initiates the drag operation in the separate app shim process. (f36a3d1)
  • On Windows, icon contrast in High Contrast mode has been fixed for hovered or pressed label buttons. Icons are no longer pre-rasterized, allowing their color to be determined dynamically for better visibility. (47c1b1a)
  • The settings page at chrome://settings/autofill has been updated. It no longer redirects and its title is now "Autofill and passwords". (318e8b4) (d18fbe3)
  • A crash in the Autofill bubble manager has been fixed by adding a check to ensure a bubble can be reshown before processing it from the pending queue. (bdb8b48)
  • A potential crash on macOS related to theme preference migration has been mitigated by ensuring the preference is registered before being accessed. (1c09de5)
  • The Windows installer will now probabilistically sample installation and update failures to gather more detailed logs and crash dumps, aiding in debugging generic errors. (e1e954b)

Extensions

  • A use-after-free crash in OneTimeMessageHandler has been fixed. A WeakPtr check now ensures the handler is still alive before closing a message port after a JavaScript reply. (c3abe5e)
  • A critical crash has been fixed that occurred when using a v8::Context after it had been invalidated (e.g., by page navigation). Cleanup tasks are now performed before any JavaScript that could invalidate the context can run. (2740712)
  • As part of an ongoing modernization effort, the internal definitions for the chrome.power and extensions.offscreen APIs were migrated from the legacy JSON schema to WebIDL. (d2b1a83) (6bb9e10)
  • The implementation of the chrome.history API was extracted into its own build target, continuing the effort to modularize browser components. (872e2a6)

Testing

  • The actor-based testing utility TabManagementTool has been enhanced to support activating and closing tabs, in addition to its previous creation capabilities. (9451810)
  • A stable element identifier, kSettingsOverriddenDialogId, has been added to the dialog that appears when an extension overrides settings, making it easier to write reliable interactive UI tests for this surface. (e52af7b)
  • The UIA event waiter for accessibility testing on Windows was updated to improve its ability to watch for events that Chromium does not directly support, enhancing test capabilities. (366011b)

2026-01-27

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-01-27

Overview

This digest covers 680 commits on the main branch from January 27, 2026, with 535 identified as relevant to developers working on Chromium-based projects. The day saw an unusually high number of breaking API changes across core utilities, extensions, and UI components. Other key areas of focus include performance optimizations and security enhancements. The full range of commits is from (6bc3790) to (37969c6).

APIs and Core Internals

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The widely used base::Value class has been refactored. The nested types base::Value::Dict and base::Value::List have been renamed to base::DictValue and base::ListValue respectively. This is a fundamental change that will require updates in any code that directly uses or casts to the old types. (dddcb58)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The gfx::BufferFormat enum and related utilities have been removed from the graphics stack. Embedders interacting with graphics buffers must migrate to viz::SharedImageFormat. (abf19e4)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING In the drag-and-drop API, OSExchangeData::GetURLsAndTitles() has been removed. Code must be updated to use GetURLs(), which now returns a struct containing both URL and title information. (b503fa4)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The ui::WebDialogUIBase class has been merged into ui::WebDialogUI, removing the base class. Embedders inheriting from WebDialogUIBase must update their code to inherit directly from ui::WebDialogUI. (3012c6e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING base::BindOnce and base::BindRepeating are now marked with [[nodiscard]]. This may cause new compiler warnings or errors in projects that call these functions and discard the result, potentially breaking builds that use -Werror. (6af8098) (dedcec8)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public API for BackgroundTaskScheduler now accepts TaskIds enum types directly instead of int. Callers must update their method calls to pass the enum. (2dab51d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The virtual method MakeBitstreamOutputStream has been removed from the public AudioManagerBase class. Its creation is now guarded by the ENABLE_PASSTHROUGH_AUDIO_CODECS build flag, effectively removing it from default builds. (17535aa)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING In the PartitionAlloc memory allocator, PartitionRoot has been refactored from a struct to a class and its member variables renamed. This will break any code that directly accesses members of PartitionRoot. (4c9f31d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The low-level allocator shim API in base/allocator/partition_allocator/ has been updated. Allocation function signatures now accept an additional size_t alloc_token argument, breaking embedders who use this shim layer directly. (150979b)
  • The ProfileManager now defers asynchronous profile loading until after the initial profile is created to prevent race conditions during startup. (7c0f1c6)

Extensions and Web Apps

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The method BrowserWindow::GetExtensionsContainer() has been removed from the public BrowserWindow interface. Embedders must now use the static method ExtensionsContainer::From(Browser&) to access the extensions container. (6d09036) (b2aefa0)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public API for ExtensionsMenuDelegateDesktop has been changed. GetActiveWebContents() was removed, and the OnActiveWebContentsChanged observer method no longer passes a content::WebContents* parameter. (ec3c934)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The signature for ExtensionTabUtil::GetTabIdForExtensions() was changed from taking a WebContents* pointer to a WebContents& reference, requiring updates for all callers. (9c932e3)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Public headers for the Split Tab feature were moved from components/tabs/public/ to new locations in components/browser_apis/tab_strip/ and components/split_tabs/, requiring embedders to update include paths. (5ae8298) (d9e6fad)
  • Performance of extensions using content scripts at document_start is improved by compiling the injected JavaScript on a background thread. (101c2fe)
  • The Web App Launch API for file handling was updated to align with spec changes, adding a targetURL attribute to launch parameters. (9e287c6)

Networking and Security

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The PrivateNetworkRequestPolicyOverride enum in content::ContentBrowserClient was renamed to LocalNetworkAccessRequestPolicyOverride. (c335bed)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The core networking Mojo interface network::mojom::URLLoaderFactoryParams was changed. The process_id field is now a network::OriginatingProcess class instead of a raw int, improving type safety. (ae20224)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING In the public blink::mojom::CommonNavigationParams Mojo struct, the has_user_gesture field has been renamed to has_possibly_filtered_user_gesture to better reflect its behavior. (e340c57)
  • On Windows, a new method SetIsolationSecurityAttribute was added to the RestrictedToken class, allowing a token's default DACL to block access from processes without a specific security attribute. (37969c6)
  • On Windows, the sandbox hooks for Code Integrity Guard (CIG) were refactored to remove the use of IPC, improving security and performance. (46c377e)
  • A new DevTools Protocol domain, SmartCardEmulation, was added to allow emulation of the PC/SC backend for testing the Web Smart Card API. (6066af9)
  • Auto-revocation of permissions for "spammy" notifications is now enabled by default. (a269e9e)

Browser UI and Features

  • ⚠️ BREAKING APIs in BrowserView and ToolbarView have been refactored to make the distinction between LocationBar and LocationBarView more explicit. This changes method signatures for accessing the location bar and will break embedder code that interacts with the omnibox container. (35c8239)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The chrome://user-actions WebUI page and its handler have been moved from chrome/browser/ui/webui/ to components/webui/user_actions/. This change of file paths will break builds that directly include the old headers. (69880ac)
  • The browser now handles google-chrome:// scheme navigations internally, improving robustness and performance by bypassing OS-level protocol handlers. (e440cf0)
  • On macOS, the maximum size for an application's Info.plist data was increased, fixing installation failures for PWAs with large icon sets. (89eb883)
  • The feature to display a user-actionable error when the bookmark sync limit is exceeded (kSyncShowBookmarksLimitExceededError) has been enabled by default. (41a8a5e)

Autofill

  • ⚠️ BREAKING ChromeAutofillClient no longer inherits from content::WebContentsObserver. Code that relied on this inheritance for casting or observing WebContentsObserver events directly from a ChromeAutofillClient instance will break. (ce9fc87)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public method FormFieldData::IsFocusable() in the Autofill API has been renamed to is_focusable(), affecting any code that inspects form field properties. (321d1a0)

Performance

  • A new PriorityBoostLoadingBrowserNetworkPolicy was implemented to improve startup performance. It disables process priority boosting on startup and then dynamically enables it for the network process and renderers only when a page is actively loading. (23517c6)
  • Two features supporting renderer process reuse (kTrackEmptyRendererProcessesForReuse and kProcessPerSiteUpToMainFrameThreshold) have been enabled by default to improve performance and memory usage. (6e70d31)
  • To improve startup performance on Windows, the task runner for BatteryLevelProviderWin was changed to BEST_EFFORT priority, preventing it from loading certain DLLs during the critical startup path. (054bd0b)

Build and Test Infrastructure

  • ⚠️ BREAKING A major file renaming effort was undertaken to standardize test file suffixes from _unittests.cc and _browsertests.cc to _unittest.cc and _browsertest.cc, respectively. This will break custom build configurations or scripts that explicitly reference the old filenames. (29c9e32)
  • The Sync browser test framework was updated to use a real EmbeddedTestServer with a FakeServer backend, enabling more comprehensive end-to-end testing of the network stack. (0304860) (0b52517)
  • On Windows, the installer's logic for moving and deleting directories was made more robust by using a new FileConductor to perform pseudo-transactional operations, increasing resilience against file-locking issues. (cde7f49)

2026-01-25

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-01-25

Overview

This digest covers 35 commits from (f5a673b) to (26e2892). After filtering for changes relevant to Windows/macOS embedders and ignoring internal rendering and OS-specific changes, this report highlights 1 major breaking change in a public security API and a large-scale refactoring of core data types.

Security

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The virtual method ShouldEnforceNewCanCommitUrlChecks has been removed from the public content::ContentBrowserClient interface. This method was a temporary kill switch allowing embedders to opt out of stricter navigation security checks. Embedders who were overriding this method will now encounter a compilation error and must remove the override from their ContentBrowserClient implementation. (26e2892)

Core & Refactoring

  • A large-scale mechanical refactoring has replaced widespread usage of the legacy base::DictValue and base::ListValue classes with their modern equivalents, base::Value::Dict and base::Value::List. This affects numerous components, including Safe Browsing, enterprise features, networking services, USB device management, and testing utilities. While these individual commits are just updating call sites, the overall effort represents a significant modernization of the base::Value API that embedders should be aware of. (f5a673b) (9740839) (64f2461) (d08503f) (db21ab2) (d828964) (90a977d) (4a61748) (abbf6fa) (6d30864) (e99429c) (62db526) (c01742e) (aee83ff) (6ee1d21) (8cc2eb9) (4256b2d) (8cdebdf)

Browser & UI

  • Internal process model optimizations are being explored for initial WebUI pages, allowing them to run in a separate, more optimized process. This work is currently controlled by the kInitialWebUI feature flag and does not affect public APIs. (e4c521d) (3dec63a)

2026-01-24

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-01-24

Overview

This digest covers 89 commits from the Chromium repository, filtered to focus on changes relevant to developers building on top of the browser for Windows and macOS. This analysis excludes platform-specific changes for Android, iOS, and ChromeOS, as well as internal Blink/V8 modifications. Key updates include two breaking changes in core utilities and APIs, security hardening for the clipboard, and new enterprise policies. The first commit is (af71659) and the last is (752be1b).

Core & Base Utilities

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The signature for base::ToVector has been changed. The two-argument overload no longer has a default projection parameter. Code making single-argument calls like base::ToVector(my_range) will now resolve to a new, more efficient overload and may require updates if the previous behavior with a default projection was expected. (a2039f6)
  • The google-chrome:// URL scheme is now correctly handled on macOS when opened via Apple Events, stripping the prefix before navigation to align with behavior on other platforms. This logic was extracted into a reusable google_chrome_scheme_util utility. (d033255)
  • The webui_url_utils files have been moved from chrome/browser/ui to chrome/common to allow access from components that cannot depend on the browser UI layer. This is a non-functional refactoring. (a3db8af)
  • The signature for media::PaintCanvasVideoRenderer::ConvertVideoFrameToRGBPixels has been updated to use base::span<uint8_t> instead of a raw void* for the pixel buffer, improving type safety. (dcf4400)

Security & APIs

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: Support for the "Preview" protocol has been completely removed from the Digital Identity API. Calls attempting to use this protocol will now fail. Only the openid4vp protocol remains for bypassing the user interstitial. (a40eb38)
  • Security has been enhanced for the clipboard API. The methods ReadAvailableCustomAndStandardFormats() and ReadUnsanitizedCustomFormat() now require renderer paste permissions via IsRendererPasteAllowed(), preventing compromised renderers from reading custom clipboard formats without user activation. (67ed1f7)
  • A new enterprise policy, RestrictPdfSaveToGoogleDriveAccountsToPattern, has been added. This allows administrators to restrict which Google accounts can be used for the 'Save to Drive' feature in the PDF viewer by specifying a regex pattern. (c86d1da)

Extensions

  • A new command-line flag, --permit-dnr-rules-on-webviews, has been introduced to allow Declarative Net Request rules to apply to <webview> elements, which is disabled by default. (bfcea3d)
  • A policy validation error in M145 related to Extension install policies has been fixed. The creation of the CloudPolicyStore for this purpose is now disabled for Chrome versions below M146. (af71659)

Browser UI & Features

  • The hide delay for media session notifications has been significantly reduced from 2.5 seconds to 0.5 seconds. This change aims to more quickly hide notifications for media that is no longer controllable. (0f27921)
  • A crash in the WebUI Toolbar has been fixed by adding a null check for the BrowserWindowInterface before use. (a6d3f63)
  • In Vertical Tabs, a placeholder title will now be correctly shown for tabs that are still loading and do not yet have a title. (9c249d5)
  • A bug causing crashes in incognito mode has been fixed by disabling bitmap prefetching for the search aggregator feature, which is not active in incognito. (57efe55)

Testing & Infrastructure

  • Several UI browser tests have been made more robust by explicitly waiting for browser window visibility (f9e4c09b) and ensuring a deterministic activation order for created browser windows (2fbf651b). (f9e4c09) (2fbf651)
  • A flaky test for the Access Code Cast feature has been re-enabled after UI migration to Lit, which is expected to resolve the flakiness. (e992002)

2026-01-23

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-01-23

Overview

This summary covers 531 relevant commits out of 672 total from the main branch on January 23, 2026. The day was marked by a significant number of breaking API changes, including a major mechanical rename of base::Value::Dict and base::Value::List, numerous interface modifications in the UI, extensions, and security layers, and several file path and header relocations. The changes span from (dd4a3fe)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((dd4a3fe)) to (4b3959a)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((4b3959a)).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The widely used base::Value::Dict and base::Value::List have been renamed to base::DictValue and base::ListValue respectively. This is a mechanical but breaking change that requires embedders to update all usages of these fundamental data types from the base library. (8e25bad) (42b022b) (d673b3a)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING A new pure virtual method, GetGroupIdForSavedGroup, has been added to the public sessions::LiveTabContext interface. Embedders who implement this interface for session restoration must add an implementation for this new method. (1ae9a24)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The extensions::ManagementPolicy::Provider::UserMayInstall method signature has been changed from a synchronous bool return to an asynchronous function that uses a callback. Custom providers must be updated to the new async pattern. (b79a8c6)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING In content::ContentBrowserClient, the method ShouldOverridePrivateNetworkRequestPolicy has been renamed to ShouldOverrideLocalNetworkAccessRequestPolicy. Embedders implementing this method must update to the new signature. (2c60d0e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Several public virtual methods have been removed from the TabStripController and TabSlotController interfaces, including CanShowModalUI, ShowModalUI, and IsTabPinned. Custom UI implementations subclassing these controllers will need to be updated. (e2eb5ce)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The core browser navigation functions chrome::CanGoBack and chrome::GoBack have been modified to incorporate "back-to-opener" logic, altering the fundamental behavior of the back button. (81bc0fc)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The constructor for ReloadButton (chrome/browser/ui/views/toolbar/reload_button.h) now requires a Browser* instance, breaking custom toolbar code that directly instantiates this class. (f10c65f)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public headers split_tab_id.h and split_tab_visual_data.h have been moved from components/tabs/public/ to a new component components/split_tabs/, breaking include paths. (fd58fdc)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The content setting ContentSettingsType::DURABLE_STORAGE has been renamed to ContentSettingsType::PERSISTENT_STORAGE. Code managing this permission must be updated. (9ca1f26)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Several file access methods in the public content::ChildProcessSecurityPolicy interface (e.g., GrantReadFile) have been updated to use the strongly-typed ChildProcessId instead of an int. (4396132)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The method GetPasskeyByUserId has been removed from the public PasskeyModel interface. Consumers should now use GetPasskeys() and filter the results manually. (1f9a2d6)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The virtual method GetCurrentFormInteractionsFlowId() has been removed from the AutofillClient interface. Embedders must remove this method from their implementations. (4d268be)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public class GpuMemoryBufferVideoFramePool in //media has been renamed to MappableSharedImageVideoFramePool. Downstream code must be updated to use the new class name. (058f840)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The SetIsCrossOriginIsolated method has been removed from the public content::mojom::Renderer mojom interface, breaking code that uses it for browser-renderer communication. (6686a4a)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The borderless_url_patterns field has been removed from the WebApp protobuf definition and its corresponding C++ structs, impacting code that interacts with Web App installation or persistence. (51b7656)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public GPU IPC headers gpu/ipc/common/gpu_command_buffer_traits.h and gpu/ipc/common/gpu_command_buffer_traits_multi.h have been removed as part of a migration to Mojom. (d34682a)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public method AnyActionHasCurrentSiteAccess() has been removed from the ExtensionsToolbarViewModel public API. (ea548b0)

Browser & UI

  • The Tab Search button in the toolbar is now enabled by default, altering the default appearance of the browser's top chrome. (eeb683f)
  • UI surfaces related to Autofill and Payments have been updated to use Google Wallet branding and icons, replacing the previous Google Pay branding. (058fc79) (7c4a770)
  • The session restore mechanism now preserves the collapsed state of tab groups when a window is restored. This change also introduced a breaking API change to sessions::LiveTabContext. (1ae9a24)
  • On macOS, starting application audio capture on an app without an audio source will now generate a silent stream instead of an error, aligning its behavior with Windows. (625a098)
  • The PWA window.setResizable API now returns a promise that resolves after the window's resizability state has been successfully changed, providing more reliable behavior. (40fe819)
  • On macOS, Chrome will now exit if the first-run dialog is dismissed without acceptance. (f4d0239)
  • The google-chrome:// URI scheme is now registered on macOS for stable builds, aligning it with Windows behavior. (d735f29)

Extensions

  • A dangling pointer bug in GinPort has been fixed, improving the stability of extension messaging by ensuring pointers are cleared when a port is disconnected. (200b225)
  • The identity.launchWebAuthFlow API now supports showing an infobar in the authentication pop-up, which can provide users with more context during the flow. (0e63868)
  • A bug has been fixed to better handle errors when a user attempts to load an unpacked extension with a non-existent manifest file. (f58b141)

Security, Policy & Networking

  • The asynchronous methods for the Supervised User URL filter have been migrated to the SupervisedUserUrlFilteringService, resulting in a breaking API change for direct callers. (bd2636d)
  • On macOS, Data Controls policies are now enforced for drag-and-drop operations, preventing data exfiltration from managed contexts. (fffd35b)
  • The kill-switch feature flags kPdfEnforcements and kSandboxedFrameEnforcements have been removed, making the associated security policies for PDFs and sandboxed frames permanent. (3e3051e)
  • The LensCameraAssistedSearchEnabled policy is being deprecated in favor of the new SearchContentSharing policy. (58200f7)

Developer Tools & Testing

  • The DevTools Protocol command Target.createTarget now accepts an optional focus parameter. This allows automation tools to create a new foreground tab without bringing the entire browser window to the front. (89de898)
  • New build-time CSS linting checks have been enabled for WebUI, enforcing a consistent style (e.g., single quotes) across WebUI components. (cac5b1b)
  • Recent changes to the activity_reporter component, which communicates browser usage to the updater on Windows and macOS, were reverted, temporarily restoring previous reporting logic. (0322fa2) (dd4a3fe)

2026-01-20

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-01-20

Overview

This summary covers 407 relevant commits out of 565 total for the main branch on January 20, 2026. The day was marked by an exceptionally high number of significant breaking changes, including the complete removal of the legacy SigninManager and the "Fast Checkout" feature, requiring immediate attention from developers building on Chromium. First commit: (bfee2f8), Last commit: (6407790).

🚨 Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The legacy SigninManager and its factory have been completely removed from chrome/browser/signin. This is a major API removal affecting all desktop sign-in and identity integrations. Embedders must migrate to modern identity management patterns like IdentityManager. (ef1e523) (a4da4de)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The entire "Fast Checkout" feature has been removed from the codebase. This includes all related public APIs such as FastCheckoutCapabilitiesFetcher and its factory, breaking any integrations that relied on this functionality. (52e96c0)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public content::RenderProcessHost interface has two new pure virtual functions: IncrementPendingReuseRefCount and DecrementPendingReuseRefCount. Embedders with custom RenderProcessHost implementations must add these methods to avoid build failures. (7f786d9)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Multiple core components have had public APIs removed or altered. This includes the removal of base::HistogramFlattener, base::MakeClampedNum, components/performance_manager/graph/policies/bfcache_policy.h, and the entire components/sync/mojom directory. The base::HistogramSnapshotManager is now an abstract base class. (7cf7b7d) (db0a734) (b2f670f) (0bd08e9) (99af97b)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Significant refactoring in the media and graphics stack has resulted in several breaking changes. RenderableGpuMemoryBufferVideoFramePool has been renamed to RenderableMappableSharedImageVideoFramePool. Legacy IPC traits for several gpu:: types have been removed in favor of mojom. The media function IsApplicationAudioCaptureSupported() has been removed and replaced by IsApplicationLoopbackCaptureSupported(). (a852568) (e7bae9e) (ae2e3c4)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Several other public APIs have been removed or renamed across the browser. This includes the removal of Browser::TYPE_CUSTOM_TAB, TabUIHelper::AddCrashedStatusChangedCallback, AutofillManager::FindCachedFormsBySignature(), and the GetUpdaterPolicies and GetAppPolicies methods from updater.mojom. The password_manager::features_util::IsAccountStorageEnabled() function was also renamed to IsAccountStorageActive(). (18d3528) (1d69364) (666e87b) (9cd9faa) (9a41144)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The DownloadRequestLimiter API has been changed, removing GetDownloadState(..., bool create) and replacing it with separate GetDownloadState(...) const and GetOrCreateDownloadState(...) methods. (e4c421a)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING On macOS, the public method SetPreferredIntervalRange() has been removed from display::DisplayLinkMac and related classes, as the underlying feature is no longer planned. (3dac675)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The SkDeserialProcs API used for custom paint preview and printing has changed. fTypefaceProc was removed and replaced by fTypefaceStreamProc, which has a different signature. Custom deserialization logic will need to be updated. (d8ed25b)

APIs and Extensions

  • A new parameter has been added to content::LoadUrlParams to allow stripping extra headers on cross-origin redirects, providing more control for embedders. (216bc9f)
  • The PWA installation flow has been changed so that the <install> HTML element will now always bypass the permission prompt, treating the request as implicitly granted by the user's click. (8d4441f)
  • A bug in the chrome.webRequest API has been fixed where the onAuthRequired event was not dispatched for navigations intercepted by service workers. (6407790)
  • The chrome.tabGroups API onUpdate event will now correctly fire when a tab group's visual data (e.g., title, color) is changed. (a2234fe)
  • The autofillPrivate extension API now requires user re-authentication when changing the preference for viewing saved payment card data, enhancing security. (9fc159f)

Security and Policy

  • A UI spoofing vulnerability in Picture-in-Picture (PiP) windows has been fixed by forcing the origin URL to always render Left-to-Right, preventing misuse of Right-to-Left Override (RTLO) characters. (58a8c05)
  • On macOS, the UnexportableKeyServiceImpl now uses both the wrapped key and application tag for key lookups, preventing potential key collisions between different applications. (6adacbf)
  • A bug in the enterprise extension telemetry router has been fixed, ensuring that events from off-store extensions are correctly reported. (10db27e)

Browser UI and Features

  • The Vertical Tabs UI received several fixes for drag-and-drop rendering, group editor bubble behavior, and alignment of top container buttons. (dd67882) (f682b0e) (5cd3982) (f18ce30)
  • On macOS, several main menu commands are now correctly disabled when a window has an attached sheet (modal dialog), preventing user confusion and potential errors. (125bb3d)
  • The WebUIToolbar now supports TrackedElement identifiers, which improves its integration with automated UI testing frameworks. (f9a42ce)
  • The side panel bookmarks list will no longer reset its scroll position when the list is modified, improving the user experience. (37c2a87)

Core Internals and Performance

  • Trace events for base::WaitableEvent and base::ConditionVariable have been improved to capture the caller's source location, making performance analysis of thread synchronization more actionable. (35892d8)
  • The kMojoInlineMessagePayloads feature flag has been removed, enabling this performance optimization by default for all users. (6b62321)
  • base::BindOnce has been improved to support binding to static operator() overloads, improving its flexibility. (933f4db)
  • A large internal rename of "Supervised User" to "Family Link" was performed across the codebase to align with current product branding. (97b35f3)

2026-01-19

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-01-19

Overview

This summary covers 436 commits on the main branch for January 19, 2026, with 267 identified as relevant to developers working on Chromium-based projects. This day saw several significant breaking changes, including the removal of the legacy TabLoader class, the deletion of several base::HistogramBase helper functions, and a behavioral change to Incognito enterprise policies. The day's activity spans from commit (7b049db) to (a1a578a).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The legacy TabLoader class and its helpers (TabLoaderDelegate, TabLoaderTester, SessionRestorePolicy) have been removed from chrome/browser/sessions. Embedders relying on TabLoader for custom session restore logic must migrate to the new mechanism managed by BackgroundTabLoadingPolicy. (7eca812)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public API helper functions AddScaled(), AddKilo(), and AddKiB() have been removed from base/metrics/histogram_base.h. Projects using these must now implement scaling logic manually before calling AddCount(). (9b7ede3)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING A significant behavioral change was introduced for Incognito mode enterprise policies. The IncognitoModeUrlAllowlist policy can now override an IncognitoModeAvailability policy set to 'disabled', forcing it to 'enabled'. This may cause Incognito mode to become available unexpectedly in managed environments. (01a54f3)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public header child_process_id.h has been moved from content/public/browser/ to content/public/common/. Code including this header directly must update its include path to avoid build failures. (0f00d98)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The supervised_user::UrlFormatter class has been removed from components/supervised_user as part of a refactoring, breaking any code that used this public class. (18386c6)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING In the public VR service mojom interface, an enum value was renamed from SUBMIT_AS_MAILBOX_HOLDER to SUBMIT_AS_TEST. This signature change will break clients using this interface. (0a15604)

Browser & Session Management

  • A significant but reverted change began migrating the core BrowserListObserver to a new BrowserCollectionObserver. The change was reverted on the same day due to test failures. (25c87a7) (5013988)
  • The BackgroundTabLoadingPolicy was refactored to use the standard Performance Manager ownership system instead of a custom singleton, aligning it with modern practices. (fd30d10)
  • Usage of TabStripModel in contextual_tasks was replaced with the platform-agnostic TabListInterface to improve code sharing. (3ea620d)

UI & UX

  • The experimental Vertical Tabs feature received several improvements, including layout manager fixes and support for horizontal animations when adding or removing pinned tabs. (db67e0e) (17b5a4e) (8357c88)
  • Zoom controls are now centrally disabled for TopChrome WebUIs (e.g., Omnibox popup) to ensure they always render at 100% zoom. (d78cc4f)
  • On macOS, a bug causing incorrect WebContents focus events in PWAs when using the Find in Page control has been fixed. (99c4920)
  • The FluentOverlayScrollbar feature flag was removed, and its functionality is now merged into the main OverlayScrollbar flag. (3139933)

Security & Identity

  • The kWebAuthnPasskeyUpgrade feature flag was removed, as the feature is now stable and enabled by default. (eb53ce0)
  • WebAuthn now supports the CTAP2.2 hmac-secret-mc extension, improving PRF-on-Create support for certain authenticators. (9ad0302)
  • The WebAuthn immediate authentication dialog will now display the password source if there is a mismatch, improving user clarity. (7950611)
  • The kBiometricAuthenticationBeforeFilling preference was made read-only to prevent modification via developer tools. (040eb92)

Services & Components

  • The internal SQL library was enhanced with new BindTime() and BindTimeDelta() methods on sql::Statement, providing a standardized way to serialize time-based values. (dc42eac)
  • The WebHistoryService was updated to support a new deletion API, controlled by the kWebHistoryUseNewApi feature flag. (dbc294b)
  • New metrics were added to measure the queue size of delayed network calls during the sign-in process, helping diagnose network state discrepancies. (59cd1e9)
  • The password manager's sync bridge was updated to use absl::flat_hash_set for improved performance. (43a67e5)

Testing & Infrastructure

  • A race condition in ChromeDriver that could lead to a crash has been fixed by adding proper locking when a command is processed while its associated webview is being disposed. (583277c)
  • The build system was updated to ensure the target_platform is always passed to grit actions, fixing issues with remote builds. (f720f89)
  • The test fixture for MutableProfileOAuth2TokenServiceDelegate was parameterized to improve test coverage for access token fetching logic. (27bb20b)

2026-01-18

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-01-18

Overview

This digest covers 8 commits, with analysis focusing on 2 changes relevant to browser-level APIs, extensions, and core utilities after filtering out platform-specific, rendering, and localization updates. The review identified one major breaking change involving the removal of a core utility function from the base library and a significant refactoring within the extensions subsystem.

Base Library

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The utility function base::Contains and its corresponding header base/containers/contains.h have been removed from the codebase. The commit message indicates all internal callers were migrated. Projects built on top of Chromium that used this public helper function will need to be updated to use alternatives, such as a container's native .contains() method or std::find. (4e6249c) (73901c6)

Extensions

  • InstallStageTracker, a class for monitoring extension installation progress, has been refactored out of //chrome and moved into the //extensions component. While no behavioral changes are intended, this file relocation may require downstream projects to update their build configurations and include paths. (7bdf603)

2026-01-17

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-01-17

Overview

This digest covers 40 commits from (9d7e320) to (93f4b94). After filtering for changes relevant to Windows/macOS embedders and excluding internal refactoring, rendering, and mobile-specific updates, we highlight a significant behavioral change in the Extensions API. Other notable changes include several UI bug fixes on macOS, improvements to PWA installation logic, and updates to the build toolchain.

Extensions API

  • ⚠️ BREAKING Promise-based signatures for extension APIs are now enabled in all contexts, removing previous restrictions. For Manifest V2 extensions, this changes the behavior of API calls that omit a callback: they will now return a Promise instead of undefined. This may also affect message port lifetime, as ports may remain open longer to await potential promise resolution, which could impact extensions relying on the previous synchronous-like behavior. (3e0fd5a)

Browser UI & Interaction

  • Fixed a bug on macOS where menus opened via keyboard shortcuts with multiple modifiers would close immediately upon key release. The fix ensures that only key *press* events, not key *release* events, can cancel a menu. (a890d63)
  • Corrected an issue where the side panel's resize handle could disappear incorrectly while it still had focus due to mouse hover interactions. (b904ef8)
  • The browser window title on macOS is now correctly left-aligned to match platform conventions. (826a136)
  • Internal improvements to CADisplayLinkMac ensure the current screen refresh interval is used for VSync parameter calculations in edge cases, improving accuracy on macOS. Unused class members were also removed. (9d7e320)

Web Applications (PWA)

  • A bug in the WebAppInstallFinalizer has been fixed where incoming sync-based installs would not correctly overwrite an existing app that was suggested for migration. This ensures sync installs have the expected priority. (8dd1ffb)

Enterprise & Policy

  • Improved test coverage for OIDC (OpenID Connect) policy sign-in services, enhancing reliability for enterprise enrollment flows. (93f4b94)

Build System & Tooling

  • The Clang and Rust toolchains have been updated to newer versions. (3b424da)
  • Fixed an issue in the Rust build configuration where dependencies were passed redundantly on the command line and in a response file (.rsp), primarily affecting the Windows toolchain. (837afbb)

2026-01-16

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-01-16

Overview

This daily summary covers 415 relevant commits out of 615 total, focusing on changes impacting developers building on Chromium for Windows and macOS. The day saw an unusually high number of breaking changes across core APIs, networking, UI, and extensions, requiring significant attention from embedders. Notable non-breaking changes include a new WebUI histogram API and the integration of a 'back-to-opener' navigation feature. Commits in this range span from (fd32a8d)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((fd32a8d)) to (0ddfda3)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((0ddfda3)).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The base::MemoryPressureListener interface has been updated. The OnMemoryPressure method signature now receives (old_level, new_level) instead of a single pressure level, breaking all existing implementations of this core listener. (8c3705f)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The Permissions API has been significantly refactored. PromptOptions is no longer part of PermissionRequest and is now passed as a direct parameter to methods like Accept(), Deny(), and OnPromptResolved(). This breaks method signatures on the public permissions::PermissionRequestManager and permissions::PermissionsClient interfaces. (7688ee1) (5d3a4e1) (b47e74e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING A new virtual method, GetEndpointForDrag, has been added to the public content::ContentBrowserClient interface. Embedders must implement this method to ensure correct enforcement of drag-and-drop security policies. (f29965a)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public WebNavigationParams API has been changed. It now uses a new AgentClusterKey structure instead of several booleans, breaking embedders who construct or inspect these parameters during navigation. (c5a1fcd)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The gpu::GLHelper class and the corresponding public API method GetGLHelper() have been removed from blink::WebGraphicsContext3DProvider. Embedders relying on this for GPU context access will be broken. (e67fa28)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Support for the Versatile Video Coding (VVC) codec has been removed. This includes the removal of VVCPROFILE_* enumerators from the public media::VideoCodecProfile and gpu::VideoCodecProfile APIs. (bdb67c3)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The legacy TabLoader-based session restore mechanism and its associated feature flag BackgroundTabLoadingFromPerformanceManager have been completely removed. All tab restoration now uses the Performance Manager path. (cf33d9c) (3f0d5d7)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The static ExtensionAssetsManager::GetInstance() method has been removed. Embedders must now use ExtensionsBrowserClient::Get()-&gt;GetAssetsManager() to access this functionality, requiring code changes. (9a7acb2)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Several public virtual functions have been removed from the TabDragContext interface (chrome/browser/ui/views/tabs/dragging/tab_drag_context.h), which will break custom tab dragging implementations. (78933f9)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public methods TabStripModel::SetTabGroupNeedsAttention and TabStripModelObserver::OnTabGroupNeedsAttentionChanged have been removed as part of a refactoring. (e6961ef)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The constructor signature for TabStripControlButton and its subclasses (e.g., NewTabButton) has changed. It now requires a BrowserWindowInterface* instead of a TabStripController*. (291681b)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public header content/common/process_visibility_tracker.h has been renamed to content/common/process_priority_tracker.h, which will break any code that includes the old file path. (24075b8)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING On Windows, the base::win::AccessToken API in access_token.h has changed. The method GetSecurityAttributeString has been removed and replaced with the more flexible GetSecurityAttribute method. (dbc59bc)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The deprecated helper function base::MakeCheckedNum&lt;&gt;() has been removed from base/numerics/checked_math.h. Use C++17 Class Template Argument Deduction with base::CheckedNumeric instead. (419f713)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public virtual method AliasRequiresProxyOverride has been removed from the net::ProxyDelegate interface as part of a feature cleanup. (ee52776)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The constructor signature for TurnSyncOnHelper has changed, removing the SigninManager* parameter. This breaks code that instantiates this helper for sign-in flows. (101c6d7)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Method signatures in the public extensions::InstallApproval class have been changed to use content::BrowserContext* instead of Profile*. (6c18838)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The sandboxed SQLite VFS has been moved to a new component (//components/sqlite_vfs), which modified and broke the associated persistent_cache.mojom interface. (38cb111)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The protobuf message for enterprise SaaS usage reporting was renamed and its structure was altered, which will break data ingestion pipelines that parse this report. (f9e2d26)

Browser UI & Features

  • The "back-to-opener" feature is now integrated with the main browser back button. The button will be enabled and trigger this action if there is no other navigation history to go back to. (9bdc317)
  • The TabListInterfaceObserver has been extended with new methods: OnTabMoved to notify of tab reordering and OnTabListDestroyed to allow for proper cleanup. (bc04ad2) (ee2eb14)
  • A skeleton for a new chrome://skills dialog and page handler has been added, laying the groundwork for a future feature. (e97ce33)
  • Functionality for launching Chrome in the foreground on Windows startup has been implemented behind the kLaunchOnStartup feature flag. (a5d750f)
  • On macOS, zoom gestures like pinch-to-zoom are now disabled for the WebUI toolbar to prevent unintentional resizing of the UI. (1ce290d) (cd0ceae)
  • A user action is now logged when a user switches between horizontal and vertical tab strip modes, improving usage metrics. (28a80f6)

APIs & Extensions

  • A new, lightweight chrome.histograms API has been introduced for WebUI pages to log UMA histograms. This API does not require chrome.metricsPrivate or extra Mojo interface registration. (9044a5b)
  • The immutability of an extension's manifest is now strictly enforced by promoting DCHECKs to CHECKs in Extension::SetManifestData, enhancing security. (40393e3)
  • Metrics have been added to chrome://inspect to track the usage of various remote debugging features. (1e0ca04)
  • The documentation for the sidePanel.close() method was updated to clarify its behavior when only a global side panel is open. (a559130)

Networking & Security

  • Enterprise Data Controls policies are now enforced for drag-and-drop operations on desktop platforms, preventing sensitive data from being dragged out of the browser against policy. (f29965a)
  • HTTP cache encryption logic has been improved. Ownership of the CacheEncryptionProvider was moved to ProfileNetworkContextService to manage a master encryption key stored in profile preferences. (2fd5f4d)
  • A new histogram (Settings.SearchEngines.GoogleIconMismatches) has been added to help quantify the prevalence of third-party search engines that spoof the Google icon in settings. (1c9edd4)
  • The QUICHE library was rolled, bringing updates to QUIC and HTTP/2 session handling. (9126550)
  • Several features related to automated password changes have been enabled by default, improving the user experience for password security. (24d48c4)

Core Internals & Build System

  • The Rust toolchain on Windows was updated to pass flags via response files (.rsp) to avoid hitting command-line length limits during builds. (b6fec29)
  • The PageContentScreenshotService was moved from //chrome/browser to the more generic //components/page_content_annotations as part of a larger modularization effort. (6ec6966)
  • On Windows, the BrowserUpdaterClient was updated to use a Mojo proxy to query updater state and policies, improving robustness. (e3464dd)
  • An unsafe buffer operation in traced_value_proto_writer.cc was fixed, and an unsafe wcslen call was replaced with wcslen_s in Windows IME code to improve memory safety. (543aa22) (988a7a6)
  • CSS lint checks have been integrated into the standard WebUI build process, enforcing code quality for UI components. (c2b3d5f)

2026-01-15

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-01-15

Overview

This summary covers 665 commits to the Chromium main branch on January 15, 2026, with 483 commits filtered as relevant to developers. The changes span from the first commit ((71ba106)) to the last ((de06b69)). The day was marked by a significant number of breaking changes across APIs for WebApps, Sync, Autofill, Permissions, and Storage, requiring attention from embedders. Notable additions include new browser closing helpers and an observer for extension install policies.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING Public API Removal (WebApps): The public method WebAppRegistrar::IsInstallState() has been removed. Embedders must migrate to GetInstallState() to check if a web app is installed. (e849b33)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Public API Signature Change (FedCM): The IdentityRequestDialogController::ShowAccounts() method signature was changed to include a new completion_callback parameter. Custom FedCM dialog controller implementations will fail to compile without this update. (4229dca)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Public Interface Change (UI): New pure virtual methods (e.g., IsVisible, Bounds) have been added to the public LocationBar interface. Embedders with custom LocationBar implementations must implement these methods to avoid build failures. (da5f63b)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Public API Removal (Sync): The virtual method SyncEngine::GetCacheGuidsForAllGaiaIds() has been removed. Embedders should now use the static method SyncTransportDataPrefs::GetCacheGuidsForAllGaiaIds(). (5f70ce6)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Public API Removal (Autofill): The last_modifier_id() and set_last_modifier_id() methods have been removed from the public AutofillProfile class as part of a data model cleanup. (870f6b1)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Public API Signature Change (Permissions): The PermissionContextBase::NotifyPermissionSet method signature has been changed to require a PromptOptions parameter. Custom permission context subclasses must be updated. (adc93e0)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Public API Removal (Autofill): Several deprecated preferences have been removed from the public header components/autofill/core/common/autofill_prefs.h. Code referencing these obsolete preferences will fail to build. (352a82c)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Public API Signature Change (Storage): Methods for opening and destroying DOM storage databases, such as AsyncDomStorageDatabase::Open, now take a single database_path instead of separate directory and name arguments. Direct usage of these storage component APIs will require code changes. (d3e4a65)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Behavioral Change (Security): Strict handle checks for sandboxed processes on Windows are now enabled by default. This security hardening may cause crashes or unexpected behavior in embedder code that is not compatible with stricter validation. (3e7aecf)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Configuration Change (Enterprise): Enterprise policies for SaaS usage reporting have been renamed (e.g., to SaasUsageReportingDomainUrlsForBrowsers). Configurations using the old policy names must be updated to maintain functionality. (4843964)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING File Path Change (On-Device Translation): The translator.h header has been moved from chrome/browser/on_device_translation/ to components/on_device_translation/. Any code including this header from its old location will fail to build. (929002a)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Mojo Interface Change (Graphics): The gfx::mojom::HDRMetadata mojo interface was changed. The agtm_serialized byte array was replaced with a structured agtm field of type skia.mojom.SkHdrAdaptiveGlobalToneMap, breaking consumers of this interface. (5c20df3)

APIs and Extensions

  • New helper functions, chrome::CloseAllBrowsersWithProfile and chrome::CloseAllBrowsersWithIncognitoProfile, have been added to browser_finder.h, providing a more robust way to close windows for a specific profile. (a193675)
  • The ExtensionInstallPolicyService now includes an Observer class, allowing consumers to be notified when extension-related enterprise policies change. (0bd6c80)
  • The extension update mechanism has been improved to automatically repair corrupted extensions by forcing a re-installation, even if the version number is unchanged. This resolves an earlier rollback of the same feature. (81be1d0) (77291cd)
  • The SessionRestoreObserver interface has a new virtual method, OnSessionRestoreMetadataChanged, to notify observers of changes to session restore metadata. (028f671)
  • On macOS, the public ContentMainDelegate interface has a new virtual method, ShouldRunContentMain(), as part of a refactoring of the application shim initialization process. (b6a3df7)
  • The state management for the extensions toolbar button has been refactored from the view into the ExtensionsToolbarViewModel, changing the public API of these UI classes by moving the State enum and adding GetButtonState(). (1de8411)

Security and Networking

  • A new EcdhKeyExchange class was introduced in remoting/base to establish symmetric keys for encrypting P2P control channels, enhancing security for features like session recording. (7b8d1bd)
  • Trace events have been added to key parts of the network stack, including cookie management and HTTP transactions, to improve performance profiling capabilities. (faceaea)
  • The WebAuthenticationMicrosoftSoftwareUnexportableKeyProvider feature flag for WebAuthn on Windows has been removed. The provider's behavior is now enabled by default and can no longer be disabled via this flag. (aef72c6)
  • A use-after-free bug in the RenderWidgetHostViewAura destruction path on Windows has been fixed. (01c3da8)

Browser UI and Features

  • The global-media-controls-updated-ui feature flag has been removed, making the updated Global Media Controls UI the default on desktop platforms. (6153529)
  • The commerce "product specifications" feature and its associated chrome://product-specifications WebUI have been completely removed from the codebase. (8babf9a)
  • A new "back-to-opener" feature has been implemented, allowing users to navigate back to the opening tab when a newly opened tab is closed. (e9d972c)
  • The chrome://updater internal page on Windows and macOS can now display the updater's current state and applied policies via new methods on BrowserUpdaterClient. (b1d8ebf) (6fadf43)
  • The TabStripControlButton has been updated to support custom colors for hover and ripple effects, enabling more flexible UI theming. (ea742a4)
  • The unlaunched zero-copy-tab-capture feature and its associated flag have been removed from the codebase. (4eb688c)

Infrastructure and Tooling

  • The third-party zip library was upgraded from version 6.0.0 to 7.1.0. (2df3608)
  • In crypto, the Aead::Init() method was deprecated in favor of a new constructor that takes the key directly, simplifying lifetime management. (9989ab0)
  • GPU detection logic on Windows was improved to correctly distinguish between the hardware-accelerated XBox adapter and the software-based WARP adapter, preventing incorrect reporting of software rendering. (bbe3736)
Page 1 of 2
